fixtures.subject: Dinner party on 10/4
fixtures.body:
Hello David,
Just had to circle back and tell you again how remarkable the meal was that you prepared for us a few weeks ago.  Although we thanked you that evening, I have thought about that beautiful feast many times.  Every
single bite was outstanding!  The taste was so memorable, the explanation you gave so interesting, and your humble kindness extraordinary.  Not to mention that you left the kitchen cleaner than when you found it!
Endless thanks for all of it!  I cannot imagine how it could have been better.  You are truly in a league all your own.
Wishing you the very best,  with warmest regards,
Sue Trischman

Thanks very much for your time,Â Jonique MonteÂ

fixtures.subject: Re: Inquiry for your services
fixtures.body:
Hey Jonique,
Thank you so much for your inquiry! I'm actually under some bizarre timing
right now because I am in middle of moving. With that said I would still
love to take this on! As these dates do fall on the end of the month, if
anything unfortunate comes up I do have my partner that could easily take
over. I'm sure that I'm oversharing a bit but I'd rather to be transparent
right off the bat. ð§âð³
Below is my pricing and every menu is custom. All I ask is that you provide
me any strong dislikes/favorites + allergies. Then I can start building out
a menu towards your style. Please feel free to leave any additional
information such as the occasion, preferences, etc.
Couples (Two Guests)
3 Courses â $200/person ($400 total)
4 Courses â $250/person ($500 total)
5 Courses â $300/person ($600 total)
Large Tasting Menu (8â15+ courses) â Custom rates
- Chef Ferragamo

fixtures.body:

Thank you for the inquiry and patience with my response! I certainly do
dinners in Haverhillâitâs actually my hometown, so itâs always fun to
visit. The 13th works for me, and Iâd love to start planning your
cannabis-infused dinner. Iâll make options based on your preferences.
Feel free to reach out if you have any other details to discuss. ð¨âð³ð
Best,
Chef Ferragamo
PF Private Chef

fixtures.subject: Re: March 3 Dinner
fixtures.body:
Hey man! I think I've narrowed it down! To our top 4!
I don't know what you need for equipment, but I figured I'd tell you what I do have so you know what you'll need to bring! So I have an electric oven/stove top, a vitamix, a kitchen aid stand mixer, an instapot pressure cooker/air fryer that also can sous vide! Plus normal kitchen stuff like rubber spats, whisks spoons and stuff.
Also, I just wanted to verify, in the subject of the email you wrote March 3 Dinner, but it is for March 11! Sorry for being a pain in the ass, I just wanted to make sure!
> Slow-Braised, Caramelized Fennel
> Tempura Cauliflower Rice

fixtures.subject: Airbnb Private Dinner in Naples, Maine - Friday the 31st - Food Preferences
fixtures.body:
Hey Chef,
It was nice chatting with you on text today.  Just to be clear, as the week
goes on and you're not quite feeling up to it, don't be shy, it won't kill
us to go out to a restaurant.  Anyway, here's our general preferences.
Overall and generally speaking, we're adventurous diners, not still beating
cobra heart adventurous, but like to try new things for sure.  As far as
differing cultural flavors, etc., we're open, but we both dislike curry.
For meats, we like to avoid turkey, pork (except bacon as a flavoring /
accessory), swordfish and octopus.  No organ meats.  We particularly like
beef and lobster.  Open to game meats to a limited / tame degree.  And
since we're Maine tourists, we would particularly like lobster in the mix.
For health reasons no raw meats / fish for me personally, but Nicole (my
wife) can.  While I can not have blatant raw, I can have meats prepared as
rare as you might seem fit / best.
On the vegetable / starch side, we're game for anything.  Also open to any
vegetarian / vegan dishes you might like to offer.
For desserts, almost anything.  We love cakes, cookies, tarts, ice cream,
but Nicole doesn't like things like panna cotta, creme brulee, and like
textured items.  And nothing way way out there like duck feet ice cream.
For the cocktail, we enjoy creative bourbon sours or old fashioneds.  We
also like fancy martini / vodka like drinks.  But really open to anything,
not super experienced with nicely prepared cocktails.
Please let me know how I can pay you.
This is going to be so fun.  Thank you!
Good night.
Mike

My pricing is 160/per person. ð¨âð³

of time. Not sure how it works, first time for 10 year anniversary!

anything I'd think of

Lyndsae

fixtures.body:

  My apologies for the inconvenience, but I misread my schedule and will be
catering a wedding on this day. Are you available next week or Sunday? I
know you're staying at an airbnb this weekend so I hope it can work! I
would be happy to travel to your home if need be.
All the best,
Chef David

fixtures.subject: Dinner
fixtures.body:
Good Morning,
I had sent a message earlier this week but was not sure if it got to you so
figured I would just email.  I was wondering if you had availability for
01/24/26 (Saturday) around 4-5 to prepare a 40th Birthday dinner for my
wife.  We will be staying at an Airbnb for the weekend.  It is located at
75 Regent Hill Rd Conway, NH 03818.  It will be for 2 people. I am
interested in the THC infused options but don't know if that is available.
Regards,

dates.context: s wondering if you had availability for 01/24/26 (Saturday) around 4-5 to prepare a 40th

fixtures.subject: Re: Dinner
fixtures.body:
Hey Chris,
I did see your dinner inquiry from my website, thanks for reaching out! I
appreciate your patience in my response time.
I do have that date open at the moment. We can certainly get you marked
down for a booking. Every menu I make is custom so just give me about 7-14
days to have it finished. In which I will then send a menu draft with
several options for you to choose from. Normally our guest all eats the
same thing but I can definitely be flexible.
I have a connection for fresh truffles, I think it would be fantastic to
include this as a surprise for your wife. Let's keep it secret maybe! I'll
keep the upcharge as low as I can for truffles. Ive also got a creme brulee
recipe she'll love for the fall.
Thanks for answering all the questions in the form, it will make my menu
building a lot easier. Please let me know which proteins you prefer to see
on the menu (for exmaple: beef, lamb, scallops,) whatever you both like
best. I'll make sure to provide options that best suit you. Also, please
provide any allergies your party maybe have. Thank you!
If there's anything you'd like to add just include it in your response. If
you'd like to set up a phone call, just let me know when you are free.
- Chef Ferragamo
This is my pricing elow:
Couples (Two Guests)
3 Courses â $200/person ($400 total)
4 Courses â $250/person ($500 total)
5 Courses â $300/person ($600 total)
Large Tasting Menu (8â15+ courses) â Custom rates
Specials & Packages
Two-Day Private Dinner for Two â $900 total

I would like to choose the 4 course meal and we are ok having the same menu
Great news on the fresh truffles!
For protein I would choose the beef as she is not a big seafood eater
She also loves garlic in pretty much everything so some options with that
would be nice
Talk to you soon

fixtures.subject: Re: Dinner
fixtures.body:
Hey Chris,
I want to thank you again for your patience. With something this important,
being left without updates is understandably frustrating, and Iâm genuinely
sorry for that. I dropped the ball on communication here, and I appreciate
you sticking with me through it.
We are absolutely confirmed for Saturday, January 24th in Conway, and Iâm
really looking forward to cooking for you both.
I also want to properly follow up on the cannabis question you mentioned
early on. I apologize for not addressing this sooner. Iâm not able to serve
or provide THC-infused food in New Hampshire. That said, if you choose to
provide something yourself, Iâm happy to talk through how it could be
incorporated into the evening in a thoughtful way and walk your party
through how to enjoy it alongside the meal.
As a small thank-you for your patience, Iâd like to include fresh truffles
for the dinner.
Iâve left the menu options below. My general suggestion would be two
vegetable courses, one beef course, and dessert, but nothing here is rigid.
If something different sounds better to you, please donât hesitate to bring
it up. You have the final say on the menu, and Iâm happy to make
adjustments, offer recommendations, or tailor things however youâd like.
Thanks again for your understanding. Iâm really looking forward to hearing
your thoughts and making this a great night for your wife.
Best,
Chef Ferragamo
*MENU*
*Veg*
Creamy winter squash soup with caramelized pumpkin, toasted grains and
nuts, brown butter sage cream
Slow-roasted rutabaga with savory hazelnut crumb, fermented garlic honey,
apple cider, brown butter, fine herbs
Winter root vegetable carpaccio with crispy leeks, confit black walnuts,
smoked beef tallow, sumac, garlic shrub vinaigrette, confit garlic, shaved
manchego
Crispy waffle fries tossed with garlic, parmesan, and fresh shaved truffle,
served with house ketchup
-
*Protein*
Beef Wellington with elderberry and confit garlic demi, hasselback carrots,
pea tendrils, radish
Dry-aged ribeye sous vide with butter, thyme, and garlic, seared hard,
finished with confit garlic butter and toasted onions, cauliflower au
gratin, roasted pickled peppers, rosemary, parsley
Braised short rib with potatoes fondant, creamed spinach, crispy fried
garlic, horseradish crÃ¨me
-
*Dessert*
Layered chocolate cake with beet and raspberry, champagne vanilla gelato
Carrot cake French toast with maple frosting, candied walnuts, carrot cake
gelato, brown butter chai maple broth
Mead-poached pears with squash, walnut, and sablÃ© crumb, salted caramel
gelato

fixtures.subject: Re: Dinner
fixtures.body:
Thanks for your selections. We can certainly do a creme brulee and gelato
for you. If there's any flavors you both prefer, just let me know. Here are
some options:
Creme brulee flavors: vanilla beans, eggnog, pumpkin, or chocolate
The gelato will vary depending on what you pick, but im open to suggestions
too
I'll make sure there's no raspberry.
I have your truffles noted, don't you worry! I was going to include them
with the entree you picked regardless. My apologies for not implying that.

>> Best,
>> Chef Ferragamo

No need to apologize, I'm awful at communication. I can definitely make you
a potato soup no problem!
Here are the confirmed details for the dinner:
Date: Saturday, January 24, 2026
Location: Conway, NH *(please provide exact address)*
Occasion: 40th birthday dinner
Menu (4 courses):
   1. Potato soup
   2. Winter root vegetable carpaccio
   3. Dry-aged ribeye with truffles
   4. Chocolate crÃ¨me brÃ»lÃ©e with gelato
Allergies / preferences:
â No raspberries
Pricing:
â 4-course private dinner for two â $500 total
My dinners normally start around 6:00 PM, and I arrive about two hours
prior, but I can cook at whatever time works best for you.
Payment can be sent via Venmo anytime, or cash is fine day of.
Venmo: *Tap here to pay* â
https://venmo.com/code?user_id=2147263816138752647&created=1767750952
â Chef Ferragamo

forecast until Sunday
Call anytime

fixtures.subject: December Dinner
fixtures.body:
Hey Louisa,
I'm just seeing your submission. I apologize for my delayed response; I've
been experiencing some technical issues with my site.
I'll start working on a menu for you based on your criteria. My pricing is
$165 per person for parties of five or more, with an increase for smaller
gatherings. For a private initmate dinner for two, the rate is $200. I hope
this finds you well.
Looking forward to hearing from you. ð
Best regards,
David Ferragamo

fixtures.subject: Oct 18 Cannabis Dinner
fixtures.body:
Hey Shelby,
I appreciate your submission. I'm very sorry about my delayed response! The
website provider I use has been throwing my cannabis submissions in the
spam folder, this is something I'm working on fixing.
I'd love to come cook for your end of season gathering. We can certainly
always work on a collaboration also, I'm always open to this. That sounds
like fun!
I do specialize in modern new england cuisine, I will begin working on a
vegan menu for you. Please don't hesitate to leave any food suggestions or
further preferences. I can make anything you've got in mind! :)
I have you down for a 4-course meal. Please let me know if this is what you
had in mind.
- Chef Ferragamo

fixtures.subject: Infused Birthday Dinner Party
fixtures.body:
Good evening,
We came across your website and are intrigued by the experience you
provide. We are hosting a dinner party to celebrate a birthday in a few
weeks (December 7) in Pepperell, MA. There will be eight adults attending.
We are not sure how it all works, but are definitely interested in having a
fun evening with great infused food and drinks.I know that guests will be
requesting different dosing levels. Is this something that fits with your
services?
Stephen & Jenilee

dates.context: to celebrate a birthday in a few weeks (December 7) in Pepperell, MA. There will be eight

fixtures.subject: Re: Infused Birthday Dinner Party

This sounds like a blast! I'm actually already booked for Dec 7, which is a
huge bummer. I can see if my guest can swap dates since it seems like you
are already set in stone. If you have other dates, feel free to let me

fixtures.subject: Farm-to-Table Dinner
fixtures.body:
Hey Laurie,
Hope your Monday is starting off well!
Thank you for showing interest in our last month's dinner. We hadn't seen
your message until now, our apologies. Fortunately, I will post the new
event this week. I will make sure that I update you on it right away.
- Chef Ferragamo

fixtures.subject: February 19 Italian Dinner
fixtures.body:
Good morning Melissa,
We are just under two weeks out from your dinner. I wanted to recap your
options. The menu we sent you was our generic Italian menu, consisting of
many classics. After reading your requirements, I'd like to send you a
revised menu we created just for you.
I have you down for nut allergy + limited carbs. I apologize if this
question is offensive, I've met many variants of nut allergies over my 10
years. Is "Pine Nut" part of your list of allergens? Thank you very much.
Choose 4 dishes to confirm your menu. Please don't be hesitant about asking
or changing anything, I'm extremely flexible. We can create anything you're
looking forward to eating.
*Important: **Our team takes all food allergies extremely seriously. Please
be thorough with all allergies. Thank you from our team.* ð
1. Charcuterie Board - 3 meat, 3 cheese, 1 pickled item, 1 fruit, 1 spread,
1 type of cracker
2. Mushroom medley w/ ricotta stuffed zucchini
3. Chicken Pesto + gnocchi w/ zucchini
4. Zucchini, Fried Prosciutto, 5 cheese alfredo, marinara
5. Seafood stuffed Tomato, fontina w/ parsley + sherry sauce
6. Short Rib Ravioli w/ beef demi, cream of spinach, confit garlic peas
7. Saltimbocca (chicken, veal or haddock)
8. Chicken cacciatore
9. Osso Buco w/ creamy polenta, carrots
10. Stuffed pork roulade w/ brown butter sage sauce, spinach, roast pepper
11. Pear caramel, dark chocolate tart
12. Peach sabayon + pumpkin granola
13. Creme brulee + berries
14. Coffee caramel goat cheesecake + chocolate mousse + cherry
15. Apple crisp w/ cinnamon caramel gelato
My apologies for taking a few days to get back to you. We catered a
spectacular Italian event this past weekend, very much looking forward to
yours. I hope you had a great weekend! Looking forward to hearing from you.
- Chef Ferragamo

>>>>>>>>> We are just under two weeks out from your dinner. I wanted to recap your options. The menu we sent you was our generic Italian menu, consisting of many classics. After reading your requirements, I'd like to send you a revised menu we created just for you.
>>>>>>>>> I have you down for nut allergy + limited carbs. I apologize if this question is offensive, I've met many variants of nut allergies over my 10 years. Is "Pine Nut" part of your list of allergens? Thank you very much.
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> Choose 4 dishes to confirm your menu. Please don't be hesitant about asking or changing anything, I'm extremely flexible. We can create anything you're looking forward to eating.
>>>>>>>>> Important: Our team takes all food allergies extremely seriously. Please be thorough with all allergies. Thank you from our team. ð
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> 1. Charcuterie Board - 3 meat, 3 cheese, 1 pickled item, 1 fruit, 1 spread, 1 type of cracker
>>>>>>>>> 2. Mushroom medley w/ ricotta stuffed zucchini
>>>>>>>>> 3. Chicken Pesto + gnocchi w/ zucchini
>>>>>>>>> 4. Zucchini, Fried Prosciutto, 5 cheese alfredo, marinara
>>>>>>>>> 5. Seafood stuffed Tomato, fontina w/ parsley + sherry sauce
>>>>>>>>> 6. Short Rib Ravioli w/ beef demi, cream of spinach, confit garlic peas
>>>>>>>>> 7. Saltimbocca (chicken, veal or haddock)
>>>>>>>>> 8. Chicken cacciatore
>>>>>>>>> 9. Osso Buco w/ creamy polenta, carrots
>>>>>>>>> 10. Stuffed pork roulade w/ brown butter sage sauce, spinach, roast pepper
>>>>>>>>> 11. Pear caramel, dark chocolate tart
>>>>>>>>> 12. Peach sabayon + pumpkin granola
>>>>>>>>> 13. Creme brulee + berries
>>>>>>>>> 14. Coffee caramel goat cheesecake + chocolate mousse + cherry
>>>>>>>>> 15. Apple crisp w/ cinnamon caramel gelato
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> My apologies for taking a few days to get back to you. We catered a spectacular Italian event this past weekend, very much looking forward to yours. I hope you had a great weekend! Looking forward to hearing from you.
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>> - Chef Ferragamo

Thank you very much for your inquiry. I currently have this spot available.
*The chef will arrive 1.5hr prior to serving time.*
Sun, Dec 28 . 6:00 PM - 9:00 PM
36 Brilliant Way Naples, ME, 04055, US
Allergies: none
Occasion: TBD
Guest Count: 2 (assuming)
For couples, pricing is:
3 courses â $225 per person ($450 total)
4 courses â $275 per person ($550 total)
5 courses â $325 per person ($650 total)
I was wondering if you have any favorite dishes, ingredients, or flavors
you love, or if thereâs anything youâve been craving lately.
Let me know what feels right, along with any allergies or strong dislikes.
Iâm looking forward to hearing from you!
- Chef Ferragamo

fixtures.subject: Private Dinner | Sept 30
fixtures.body:
Hey Anna,
I apperciate your patience as we get your dinner together. Please
confirm/fill out the information below.
Price: $100/per person
Date: Sept 30
Location:
Serving Style: 3-Course Plated
Serving Time: (Chef will arrive 2hrs early.)
Occasion:
Allergies or Restrictions:
All payments will be made upon confirming your booking. I sent over the
discussed menu selections.
1. Lobster Bisque
2. Spiced Roasted Cauliflower + Smashed Potato w/ crispy leeks + leek puree
+ local cheese curds
3. Filet Mignon w/ Compound Butter of confit pear, garlic + oregano
w/ parsnip puree, buttered seasonal green, + radish
- Chef Ferragamo

dates.context: Re: Private Dinner | Sept 30 I believe we are all set. I bring all e

fixtures.subject: July 12 Dinner
fixtures.body:
Hey Kim,
We are just one week away from our event. This email will wrap up all
concluding details.
Please change/confirm details below, thank you.
   1.
   Date: June 12
   2.
   Location of event: Dracut, MA *please specify*
   3.
   Chef Arrival time: 12pm
   4.
   Eating time: 4pm
   5.
   Allergies: None
   6.
   Guest Count: 11
------------------------------
Menu
cheeseboard - 4 cheese, local charcuterie, potato crackers, preserved + raw
veg
scallop w/ tomato, mozzarella, basil
bison w/ eggplant, corn
almond tart w/ berries + vanilla ice cream
Day of Event
We are a small team consisting of 2-3 chefs. Our team must have time to
arrive 3-4hrs early prior to dining. Upon arrival our crew will spend a
short time unloading equipment into your kitchen. We ask that all
countertop appliances must be removed to give us a full range of counter
space. It is appreciated that you provide a clean space for us to cook, in
which we will return the same. Thank you for your cooperation.
Our team will provide all the equipment, plates and silverware. This does
not include glassware.
IMPORTANT:  Due to Covid-19, we will no longer be collecting silverware
between courses. We do ask that you prepare your guests to hold on to them.
Instead of linen napkins that we will provide, we will provide a single use
napkin that we ask the host to dispose of. Thank you for supporting these
changes as we strive to keep you and our staff safe.
Be well,
Chef Ferragamo

dates.context: July 12 Dinner Hey Kim, We are just one week aw
etails below, thank you.    1.    Date: June 12    2.    Location of event: Dracut, MA

fixtures.body: Hi David. My Address is 156 Primrose Hill Rd Dracut MA. I have 2 driveways as my house is sideways to the street. If you park in the back in front of the white fence, you will have direct acess to the Kitchen. Do you need a deposit or would you prefer cash the day of the event? fixtures.subject: Dinner Follow-up
fixtures.body:
Hey! I just wanted to let you know that I have seen your inquiry, I
apologize for my response time. It's been a very busy week of dinners. I'm
actually on my way to a service right now but just wanted to reach out.
I am available on that date. Every menu I make is custom so I will use all
of your response to make the menu. Definitely let me know if you think you
left anything out!
Hereâs the pricing for groups of three or more:
$155 per person for three courses
$185 per person for four courses
$215 per person for five courses
We also offer large tasting menus, up to 15 plus courses. This would have
different rates.
And just so you can see both formats, hereâs the couple pricing too:
$200 per person for three courses
$250 per person for four courses
$300 per person for five courses
I work closely with local farms, and I also forage and grow a lot of my own
ingredients. I try to keep the majority of each dinner farm-to-table
whenever possible, to represent the community and the season.
Looking forward to hearing from you!
- Chef Ferragamo

fixtures.subject: Dinner for two?
fixtures.body:
Hello!
My husband and I are renting an airbnb in Harrison Maine next week and I
was wondering what your price was for a weekday dinner for two? Would be a
luxury highlight of our getaway! This would be for Tuesday, Wednesday, or
Thursday of next week (15-17).
Thanks so much,
-Emily

fixtures.subject: Re: Dinner for two?
fixtures.body:
Hey Emily,
Thanks for reaching out! Iâd love to cook for you two during your stay.
All the menus I create are custom, so if you have any strong dislikes or
must-haves, feel free to let me know. I focus on progressive New
England-style cooking and use a lot of local ingredients, but Iâm happy to
make anything you're in the mood for.
Here are a few options I offer for couples:
$200 per person
3 courses â appetizer, main, and dessert
$250 per person
4 courses â salad, soup, main, dessert
$300 per person
5 courses â small bites, main, and desserts
Iâm currently free on that Wed+Thursday of that week. Let me know what you
prefer and Iâll send over a menu. ð
- Chef Ferragamo
PF Private Chef

fixtures.subject: Re: Dinner for two?
fixtures.body:
Here are some options I created for you, my apologies for the late message.
I can make any alterations or changes! I threw in one fish dish for fun.
(Please cheese 1 dish from each)
1. Salads
â¢ Wilted greens with candied walnuts, cheese curds, pickled pepper, and a
confit garlic-maple vinaigrette
â¢ Roasted beet and fennel salad with goat cheese and preserved blueberry
vinaigrette
â¢ House Caesar with charred broccolini
2. Starters
â¢ Cream of cauliflower and roasted pepper over seared cauliflower, braised
turnips, and crispy leeks
â¢ Stuffed artichokes with herb breadcrumbs, Manchego, and lemon beurre blanc
3. Mains
â¢ Braised short rib with mushrooms, Madeira, slow-cooked onions, garlic
mashed potatoes, and seasonal greens
â¢ Seared ribeye with leek butter, charred peppers stuffed with roasted
vegetables and goat cheese, crispy leeks, and braised leaf greens
â¢ Rolled beef roulade with garlic scapes and preserved summer giardiniera,
served in a slow-braised spring beef broth
â¢ Baked halibut with roasted cabbage filled with celeriac-garlic mousse,
finished with lemon brown butter and capers

Please let your number if you can. ð On Thu, Apr 17, 2025, 9:30â¯AM Emily Ulrich wrote: No allergies! You ok with an earlier dinner? Maybe aiming to eat around 5:30 or 6:00? -Emily Sent from my iPhone On Apr 16, 2025, at 6:25â¯PM, David Ferragamo wrote: ï»¿ Okay thank you! You you guys have any allergies? I'll be arriving around 1-2hrs prior, please let me know when you'd like to be served. Please follow the link for paymentð§âð³ https://www.paypal.com/qrcodes/venmocs/5c62f0f5-81d2-4176-8feb-3be978c8d541?amount=400.00&currency_code=USD&created=1744842183 On Wed, Apr 16, 2025, 2:10â¯PM Emily Ulrich wrote: All of this looks wonderful! Letâs do the wilted green salad, the stuffed artichoke appetizer, and the seared ribeye! Thank you looks excellent. Sent from my iPhone On Apr 15, 2025, at 10:07â¯PM, David Ferragamo wrote: ï»¿ Here are some options I created for you, my apologies for the late message. I can make any alterations or changes! I threw in one fish dish for fun. (Please cheese 1 dish from each) 1. Salads â¢ Wilted greens with candied walnuts, cheese curds, pickled pepper, and a confit garlic-maple vinaigrette â¢ Roasted beet and fennel salad with goat cheese and preserved blueberry vinaigrette â¢ House Caesar with charred broccolini 2. Starters â¢ Cream of cauliflower and roasted pepper over seared cauliflower, braised turnips, and crispy leeks â¢ Stuffed artichokes with herb breadcrumbs, Manchego, and lemon beurre blanc 3. Mains â¢ Braised short rib with mushrooms, Madeira, slow-cooked onions, garlic mashed potatoes, and seasonal greens â¢ Seared ribeye with leek butter, charred peppers stuffed with roasted vegetables and goat cheese, crispy leeks, and braised leaf greens â¢ Rolled beef roulade with garlic scapes and preserved summer giardiniera, served in a slow-braised spring beef broth â¢ Baked halibut with roasted cabbage filled with celeriac-garlic mousse, finished with lemon brown butter and capers On Tue, Apr 15, 2025, 4:43â¯PM David Ferragamo wrote: Sounds great! No worries, I bring everything I need. All my menus are unique, Ill send menu options within the next couple hours based on your current feedback. Im hoping to have to menu settled by Wednesday at noon to retrieve ingredients. Are there any allergies I should be aware of?Big food preferences or Dislikes I may not immediately respond if you message with these answers, but I'll take them into account by tonight's email back. Thank you! -Chef Ferragamo On Tue, Apr 15, 2025, 2:40â¯PM Emily Ulrich wrote: Weâre at 214 Kimball in Harrison. Itâs an Airbnb so we donât have a ton of basic materials but are very excited to meet you! -Emily Sent from my iPhone On Apr 14, 2025, at 1:32â¯PM, David Ferragamo wrote: ï»¿ Hey Emily, Unfortunately I'm now reserved for Wednesday. That menu would be no issue, I could certainly do that! Please let me know if your still interested in booking for Thursday. My apologies for the inconvenience. Thank you! ð§âð³ - Chef Ferragamo On Mon, Apr 14, 2025, 11:58â¯AM Emily Ulrich wrote: Hello! Wednesday night would work great for us. My husband is big on protein especially for his main, and prefers meat to fish. Iâm easy! Is there any chance we can do three courses but have it be a salad, appetizer, and main? Weâre not big dessert people! So excited! -Emily Sent from my iPhone On Apr 11, 2025, at 10:37â¯AM, David Ferragamo wrote: ï»¿ Hey Emily, Id love to reserve the spot for you. Just let me know your thoughts. - Chef Ferragamo On Wed, Apr 9, 2025, 6:13â¯PM David Ferragamo wrote: Hey Emily, Thanks for reaching out! Iâd love to cook for you two during your stay. All the menus I create are custom, so if you have any strong dislikes or must-haves, feel free to let me know. I focus on progressive New England-style cooking and use a lot of local ingredients, but Iâm happy to make anything you're in the mood for. Here are a few options I offer for couples: $200 per person 3 courses â appetizer, main, and dessert $250 per person 4 courses â salad, soup, main, dessert $300 per person 5 courses â small bites, main, and desserts Iâm currently free on that Wed+Thursday of that week. Let me know what you prefer and Iâll send over a menu. ð - Chef Ferragamo PF Private Chef On Mon, Apr 7, 2025, 2:37â¯PM Emily Ulrich wrote: Hello! My husband and I are renting an airbnb in Harrison Maine next week and I was wondering what your price was for a weekday dinner for two? Would be a luxury highlight of our getaway! This would be for Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday of next week (15-17). Thanks so much, -Emily =

fixtures.subject: Re: Dinner Booking
fixtures.body:
Hey Joyce,
I just finished up your custom menu. Thank you for your patience as I
wrapped my head around the fall flavors that will be available!!
I really hope you like the selections I created for you. Please let me know
if you have any questions or would like changes! ðð¨âð³
Please choose 1item from each to create a 4 course menu.
1st
Beet salad w/ fermented garlic honey vinaigrette + goat cheese
Kohlrabi salad w/ confit garlic vinaigrette + sourdough croutons
Apple, pecan, roasted squash, mixed greens + citrus vinaigrette
Roasted squash + charred onion terrine w/ parmesan cream + Moroccan almonds
2nd
Corn soup w/ pickled pepper + thyme cream
Creamy autumn soup + mixed veg
Roasted carrot soup + crispy marinated kale crisp
3rd
Grilled steak tips w/ crunchy potatoes + seasonal greens
Braised short rib w/ creamy rutabaga puree, roast pepper + seasonal greens.
Crispy chicken provencal + roasted root veg
Salmon over winter veg risotto, spiced maple + fresh snow pea greens
Crispy duck breast w/ stone fruit gastique, roasted cauliflower + seasonal
greens
Lobster au gratin w/ seasonal greens
4th
Pear crisp w/ salted caramel + gelato
Chocolate lava cake + seasonal fruit
Pumpkin creme brule
Hazelnut + chocolate swirled cake w/ cherry gelato

fixtures.subject: Private Chef Dinner - September
fixtures.body:
Hello David,
I hope this message finds you well and you are having a great summer! I am reaching out as I have a mini-moon booked in Naples, Maine and the Airbnb host provided your contact information regarding hiring a private chef for dinner one evening. I would love to surprise my finance with a really special dinner if you have availability while we are there. We will be staying in Naples Sept 18, 19, and 20th.Â

Congratulations on your upcoming mini-moon! I can put September 18th, 19th,
and 20th on hold for you until you decide which date works best.
I do want to apologize, as Iâm unable to conduct any cannabis-related
business in the state of New Hampshire. That said, Iâd still love to cook
for you and create a really special dinner. Does you finance have any
favorite or nostalgia foods that we could surprise them with?

â¢ 3 Courses â $200/person ($400 total)

excited to make your celebration memorable! ð§âð³

fixtures.subject: Re: Private Chef Dinner - September
fixtures.body:
That makes a lot of sense. I figured you were booking through one of those
airbnb's because I remember reading that you said you would be in Naples ð
but the wix form set Seabrook my apologies!!!
I can send over the current growing summer menu that I've been adding too.
Every menu I make is custom, so I just keep adding the summer dishes to it.
Let me know if you find anything you like! I will make some more selections
based on everything you said.
The 20th sounds great to me! I can definitely hope on a phone call, perhaps
one of these upcoming Mondays? I'm glad I could come be a part of it all.
It sounds like a very fun memorable time! Does your husband have any
favorites? Perhaps we can surprise them. Thank you!
The summer options are below, I'll make more options based on your feedback
from things you've always stated. ð§âð³
VEG / APP
â¢ SautÃ©ed snap pea & broccolini salad with brown butter vinaigrette,
manchego
â¢ Grilled stone fruit, arugula, vegan ricotta, preserved pepper vinaigrette
â¢ Summer salad with blueberry balsamic vinaigrette and fresh mozzarella
â¢ Chopped beet, spinach & charred onion salad with black pepper blueberry
vinaigrette, dried lime balm, goat cheese
â¢ Tomato gazpacho with crispy shallot oil, basil, pruned blueberry capers
â¢ Smoked peppers filled with goat cream cheese, black walnuts, lilac garlic
vinaigrette
â¢ Strawberry carpaccio with charred corn relish, goat cheese, herbs
â¢ Tomato, arugula & herb salad with charred strawberry balsamic
vinaigrette, mint, tarragon
â¢ Melon & cucumber gazpacho with local feta
â¢ Cucumber & pea salad with brown butter lemon vinaigrette, manchego
â¢ Grilled Texas toast with whipped herb goat cheese, pepper jelly, raw
zucchini salad
â¢ Grilled & charred eggplant with pickled giardiniera, basil croutons, feta
â¢ Fried pickles with fermented garlic aioli, chopped chives
â¢ Bloominâ onion with horseradish aioli, sour cream chive seasoning
MAIN
â¢ Marinated grilled NY sirloin with smoked tomato butter, creamed spinach,
crispy garlic
â¢ BBQ steak tips with buttered asparagus, braised tomatoes, grilled potato
salad
â¢ Blackberry smoked & braised short rib with grilled vegetable salad,
cornbread
â¢ Chicken roulade stuffed with tomato, basil, asparagus, crispy potato
â¢ Roasted chicken with herb jus, burnt onion, turnips, marinated cherry
â¢ Crispy duck with peach mostarda, sautÃ©ed broccolini, burnt onion, herb
broth
â¢ Haddock with herb crumb, sautÃ©ed zucchini, pea shoots, basil butter
â¢ Salmon with black pepper maple glaze, sautÃ©ed beans, herb rice
â¢ Scallops pan-seared with tomato & spinach, cauliflower parm purÃ©e
â¢ Butter-poached lobster roll with lemon chive aioli, hand-cut tossed
fries, and house slaw
DESSERT
â¢ Basque cheesecake with sweet fern crust, marinated berries
â¢ Peach custard tart with white tea gelato
â¢ Peach buckwheat buckle cake with lilac icing, cheesecake gelato
â¢ Vanilla custard with marinated stone fruit, candied black walnut
â¢ Strawberry shortcake with candied lime balm
â¢ Chocolate & hazelnut bread pudding with chocolate ganache, raspberry
â¢ Chocoflan with chocolate chip gelato
â¢ Chocolate lava cake with seasonal fruit, gelato
-Chef Ferragamo

fixtures.subject: Re: Private Chef Dinner - September
fixtures.body:
Hello,
Thank you for this information - super helpful and I am excited! My schedule this Monday, August 18th is pretty flexible - do you have a preferred time?
I did think of one thing we both love - dumplings! And we would both love to watch you prepare them. For dessert, Pat loves mocha flavor and we love chocolate mousse with some funky fruit and/or candied bacon? He loves bacon!
Everything sounds delicious! No salad for the app or veg as Pat hates it - I'm working on that one!Â
As for mains, lobster, surf and turf would be great. He does not like salmon, scallops, duck or short rib.
We love steak, squash or root veggies, potatoes...I think lobster/surf and turf would be really nice! We also loves haddock.Â
Does that help at all? This is a first for me so guidance is appreciated. Would it be possible to make something for him with cannabis? Not for me, so I totally understand if that is not possible to do a dish separate.Â
I look forward to connecting - let me know what time works best!
Thanks!Â
Elisa Perel508-737-2766

fixtures.body:
Thank you very much for your feedback. That makes my menu building a lot
more helpful and personal. We can def get him some cannabis with your meal
being effected, that's no issue.
I'll make some really good bacon, you're talking to the right guy haha I've
got everything else covered. I loved how specific you were, it lets me
really hone in on what you'd like!
Sorry for my response time. I'm around Thursday if you'd like to chat. ð
My number is 978-994-0570
Also feel free to check out my Instagram, I'm always very active. I post my
dinners and daily food stuff. It'll give you an idea of the service I
provide.

fixtures.subject: Re: Private Chef Dinner - September
fixtures.body:
Hi Dave,Â
Thank you! I am so excited! Can I call you at 5 on Thursday?Â
Thanks! ElisaÂ
    On Tuesday, August 19, 2025 at 07:47:05 PM EDT, David Ferragamo <dfprivatechef@gmail.com> wrote:
 Thank you very much for your feedback. That makes my menu building a lot more helpful and personal. We can def get him some cannabis with your meal being effected, that's no issue.Â
I'll make some really good bacon, you're talking to the right guy haha I've got everything else covered. I loved how specific you were, it lets me really hone in on what you'd like!Â
Sorry for my response time. I'm around Thursday if you'd like to chat. ðMy number is 978-994-0570
Also feel free to check out my Instagram, I'm always very active. I post my dinners and daily food stuff. It'll give you an idea of the service I provide.Â

Thank you! Elisa

  Hello,
Thank you for this information - super helpful and I am excited! My schedule this Monday, August 18th is pretty flexible - do you have a preferred time?
I did think of one thing we both love - dumplings! And we would both love to watch you prepare them. For dessert, Pat loves mocha flavor and we love chocolate mousse with some funky fruit and/or candied bacon? He loves bacon!
Everything sounds delicious! No salad for the app or veg as Pat hates it - I'm working on that one!Â
As for mains, lobster, surf and turf would be great. He does not like salmon, scallops, duck or short rib.
We love steak, squash or root veggies, potatoes...I think lobster/surf and turf would be really nice! We also loves haddock.Â
Does that help at all? This is a first for me so guidance is appreciated. Would it be possible to make something for him with cannabis? Not for me, so I totally understand if that is not possible to do a dish separate.Â
I look forward to connecting - let me know what time works best!
Thanks!Â
Elisa Perel508-737-2766

MAINâ¢ Marinated grilled NY sirloin with smoked tomato butter, creamed spinach, crispy garlicâ¢ BBQ steak tips with buttered asparagus, braised tomatoes, grilled potato saladâ¢ Blackberry smoked & braised short rib with grilled vegetable salad, cornbreadâ¢ Chicken roulade stuffed with tomato, basil, asparagus, crispy potatoâ¢ Roasted chicken with herb jus, burnt onion, turnips, marinated cherryâ¢ Crispy duck with peach mostarda, sautÃ©ed broccolini, burnt onion, herb brothâ¢ Haddock with herb crumb, sautÃ©ed zucchini, pea shoots, basil butterâ¢ Salmon with black pepper maple glaze, sautÃ©ed beans, herb riceâ¢ Scallops pan-seared with tomato & spinach, cauliflower parm purÃ©eâ¢ Butter-poached lobster roll with lemon chive aioli, hand-cut tossed fries, and house slaw
DESSERTâ¢ Basque cheesecake with sweet fern crust, marinated berriesâ¢ Peach custard tart with white tea gelatoâ¢ Peach buckwheat buckle cake with lilac icing, cheesecake gelatoâ¢ Vanilla custard with marinated stone fruit, candied black walnutâ¢ Strawberry shortcake with candied lime balmâ¢ Chocolate & hazelnut bread pudding with chocolate ganache, raspberryâ¢ Chocoflan with chocolate chip gelatoâ¢ Chocolate lava cake with seasonal fruit, gelato

On Fri, Aug 15, 2025, 8:46â¯AM Elisa Perel <elisa_perel@yahoo.com> wrote:
 Hi David, Happy Friday!Â
Thank you for the quick response! The dinner is actually in Naples, Maine - not New Hampshire - does that change anything with the cannabis dinner? We are staying at an Airbnb in Maine for the nights listed. The kitchen is gorgeous, I can send you the link if that is helpful.Â
Would you have any sample menus by chance or be available for a phone call to discuss? I am trying to decide between 3 or 4 courses. We do not have any allergies or major dietary restrictions, we love lobster, mussels, steaks, root veggies, broccoli, broccoli rabe, broccolini... I love all veggies, my partner hates "salad" but I love it. Pat doesn't like oysters, clams or shrimp - I love them all.Â
Can we plan on our last night, Sept 20th for the dinner? Let me know if you have a few minutes next week for a call and I can provide more detail.
Thank you so much! We have never done anything like this before and this will be my surprise wedding gift for my partner. He has been talking about trying to do this since the Airbnb host shared your details and we started looking through your reviews.Â
Thanks again!
ElisaÂ

 Hi Elisa,

For a private dinner for two, pricing is as follows:â¢ 3 Courses â $200/person ($400 total)â¢ 4 Courses â $250/person ($500 total)â¢ 5 Courses â $300/person ($600 total)

Hello David,
I hope this message finds you well and you are having a great summer! I am reaching out as I have a mini-moon booked in Naples, Maine and the Airbnb host provided your contact information regarding hiring a private chef for dinner one evening. I would love to surprise my finance with a really special dinner if you have availability while we are there. We will be staying in Naples Sept 18, 19, and 20th.Â
Would you be available for a private dinner for two one of those nights? My partner would also love a meal cooked with cannabis if that is available for 1 and a regular meal for the me.Â
I look forward to hearing from you!
Thank you!
Elisa Perel08-737-2766

I would love to surprise my finance with a really special dinner if you have availability while we are there. We will be staying in Naples Sept 18, 19, and 20th. fixtures.subject: Re: Private Chef Dinner - September
fixtures.body: No worries at all! We are getting married on Saturday and Iâm going through my lists! He actually mentioned you last night - saying we should look into that private chef and I ignored it! Thank you again! Iâm really excited! Elisa Sent from my iPhone On Sep 10, 2025, at 9:54â¯PM, David Ferragamo wrote: ï»¿ Lol you're good! It's been a crazy month for me, you're totally warranted for the follow-up. Just give me a couple more days. I'm really sorry On Wed, Sep 10, 2025, 7:18â¯PM Elisa Perel wrote: Hi David, I hope all is well! I just wanted to check in - do you know when youâll have menu options available? No rush, just checking in! Thanks! Elisa Sent from my iPhone On Aug 21, 2025, at 4:55â¯PM, David Ferragamo wrote: ï»¿ Hey I believe your number got cut off. Im ready to chat. ð 9789940570 On Thu, Aug 21, 2025, 12:02â¯PM David Ferragamo wrote: Works for me! ð On Wed, Aug 20, 2025, 10:06â¯AM Elisa Perel wrote: Hi Dave, Thank you! I am so excited! Can I call you at 5 on Thursday? Thanks! Elisa On Tuesday, August 19, 2025 at 07:47:05 PM EDT, David Ferragamo wrote: Thank you very much for your feedback. That makes my menu building a lot more helpful and personal. We can def get him some cannabis with your meal being effected, that's no issue. I'll make some really good bacon, you're talking to the right guy haha I've got everything else covered. I loved how specific you were, it lets me really hone in on what you'd like! Sorry for my response time. I'm around Thursday if you'd like to chat. ð My number is 978-994-0570 Also feel free to check out my Instagram, I'm always very active. I post my dinners and daily food stuff. It'll give you an idea of the service I provide. On Mon, Aug 18, 2025, 1:50â¯PM Elisa Perel wrote: Hello David, I hope you had a great weekend and enjoyed the beautiful weather! Did you have availability for a call this week? Thank you! Elisa On Friday, August 15, 2025 at 03:37:04 PM EDT, Elisa Perel wrote: Hello, Thank you for this information - super helpful and I am excited! My schedule this Monday, August 18th is pretty flexible - do you have a preferred time? I did think of one thing we both love - dumplings! And we would both love to watch you prepare them. For dessert, Pat loves mocha flavor and we love chocolate mousse with some funky fruit and/or candied bacon? He loves bacon! Everything sounds delicious! No salad for the app or veg as Pat hates it - I'm working on that one! As for mains, lobster, surf and turf would be great. He does not like salmon, scallops, duck or short rib. We love steak, squash or root veggies, potatoes...I think lobster/surf and turf would be really nice! We also loves haddock. Does that help at all? This is a first for me so guidance is appreciated. Would it be possible to make something for him with cannabis? Not for me, so I totally understand if that is not possible to do a dish separate. I look forward to connecting - let me know what time works best! Thanks! Elisa Perel 508-737-2766 On Friday, August 15, 2025 at 11:12:48 AM EDT, David Ferragamo wrote: That makes a lot of sense. I figured you were booking through one of those airbnb's because I remember reading that you said you would be in Naples ð but the wix form set Seabrook my apologies!!! I can send over the current growing summer menu that I've been adding too. Every menu I make is custom, so I just keep adding the summer dishes to it. Let me know if you find anything you like! I will make some more selections based on everything you said. The 20th sounds great to me! I can definitely hope on a phone call, perhaps one of these upcoming Mondays? I'm glad I could come be a part of it all. It sounds like a very fun memorable time! Does your husband have any favorites? Perhaps we can surprise them. Thank you! The summer options are below, I'll make more options based on your feedback from things you've always stated. ð§âð³ VEG / APP â¢ SautÃ©ed snap pea & broccolini salad with brown butter vinaigrette, manchego â¢ Grilled stone fruit, arugula, vegan ricotta, preserved pepper vinaigrette â¢ Summer salad with blueberry balsamic vinaigrette and fresh mozzarella â¢ Chopped beet, spinach & charred onion salad with black pepper blueberry vinaigrette, dried lime balm, goat cheese â¢ Tomato gazpacho with crispy shallot oil, basil, pruned blueberry capers â¢ Smoked peppers filled with goat cream cheese, black walnuts, lilac garlic vinaigrette â¢ Strawberry carpaccio with charred corn relish, goat cheese, herbs â¢ Tomato, arugula & herb salad with charred strawberry balsamic vinaigrette, mint, tarragon â¢ Melon & cucumber gazpacho with local feta â¢ Cucumber & pea salad with brown butter lemon vinaigrette, manchego â¢ Grilled Texas toast with whipped herb goat cheese, pepper jelly, raw zucchini salad â¢ Grilled & charred eggplant with pickled giardiniera, basil croutons, feta â¢ Fried pickles with fermented garlic aioli, chopped chives â¢ Bloominâ onion with horseradish aioli, sour cream chive seasoning MAIN â¢ Marinated grilled NY sirloin with smoked tomato butter, creamed spinach, crispy garlic â¢ BBQ steak tips with buttered asparagus, braised tomatoes, grilled potato salad â¢ Blackberry smoked & braised short rib with grilled vegetable salad, cornbread â¢ Chicken roulade stuffed with tomato, basil, asparagus, crispy potato â¢ Roasted chicken with herb jus, burnt onion, turnips, marinated cherry â¢ Crispy duck with peach mostarda, sautÃ©ed broccolini, burnt onion, herb broth â¢ Haddock with herb crumb, sautÃ©ed zucchini, pea shoots, basil butter â¢ Salmon with black pepper maple glaze, sautÃ©ed beans, herb rice â¢ Scallops pan-seared with tomato & spinach, cauliflower parm purÃ©e â¢ Butter-poached lobster roll with lemon chive aioli, hand-cut tossed fries, and house slaw DESSERT â¢ Basque cheesecake with sweet fern crust, marinated berries â¢ Peach custard tart with white tea gelato â¢ Peach buckwheat buckle cake with lilac icing, cheesecake gelato â¢ Vanilla custard with marinated stone fruit, candied black walnut â¢ Strawberry shortcake with candied lime balm â¢ Chocolate & hazelnut bread pudding with chocolate ganache, raspberry â¢ Chocoflan with chocolate chip gelato â¢ Chocolate lava cake with seasonal fruit, gelato -Chef Ferragamo On Fri, Aug 15, 2025, 8:46â¯AM Elisa Perel wrote: Hi David, Happy Friday! Thank you for the quick response! The dinner is actually in Naples, Maine - not New Hampshire - does that change anything with the cannabis dinner? We are staying at an Airbnb in Maine for the nights listed. The kitchen is gorgeous, I can send you the link if that is helpful. Would you have any sample menus by chance or be available for a phone call to discuss? I am trying to decide between 3 or 4 courses. We do not have any allergies or major dietary restrictions, we love lobster, mussels, steaks, root veggies, broccoli, broccoli rabe, broccolini... I love all veggies, my partner hates "salad" but I love it. Pat doesn't like oysters, clams or shrimp - I love them all. Can we plan on our last night, Sept 20th for the dinner? Let me know if you have a few minutes next week for a call and I can provide more detail. Thank you so much! We have never done anything like this before and this will be my surprise wedding gift for my partner. He has been talking about trying to do this since the Airbnb host shared your details and we started looking through your reviews. Thanks again! Elisa On Thursday, August 14, 2025 at 09:04:03 PM EDT, David Ferragamo wrote: Hi Elisa, Thank you for reaching out, I received the wix form as well. Congratulations on your upcoming mini-moon! I can put September 18th, 19th, and 20th on hold for you until you decide which date works best. I do want to apologize, as Iâm unable to conduct any cannabis-related business in the state of New Hampshire. That said, Iâd still love to cook for you and create a really special dinner. Does you finance have any favorite or nostalgia foods that we could surprise them with? For a private dinner for two, pricing is as follows: â¢ 3 Courses â $200/person ($400 total) â¢ 4 Courses â $250/person ($500 total) â¢ 5 Courses â $300/person ($600 total) If youâd like, please share any allergies, favorites, or strong dislikes so I can start shaping the menu around your preferences. Let me know which date and menu option youâd like to move forward with. Iâm excited to make your celebration memorable! ð§âð³ - Chef Ferragamo On Thu, Aug 14, 2025, 3:47â¯PM Elisa Perel wrote: Hello David, I hope this message finds you well and you are having a great summer! I am reaching out as I have a mini-moon booked in Naples, Maine and the Airbnb host provided your contact information regarding hiring a private chef for dinner one evening. I would love to surprise my finance with a really special dinner if you have availability while we are there. We will be staying in Naples Sept 18, 19, and 20th. Would you be available for a private dinner for two one of those nights? My partner would also love a meal cooked with cannabis if that is available for 1 and a regular meal for the me. I look forward to hearing from you! Thank you! Elisa Perel 08-737-2766 =

Sorry for my response time. I'm around Thursday if you'd like to chat. ð My number is 978-994-0570 Also feel free to check out my Instagram, I'm always very active. I post my dinners and daily food stuff. It'll give you an idea of the service I provide. fixtures.subject: Re: Private Chef Dinner - September
fixtures.body:
Hey I'm really sorry for making you wait. I got caught with a horrible case
of Covid and have been bedridden since we last spoke. Thank you very much
for your patience.
Below I have options I created for you, I will make sure there's a secrets
candied bacon snack and cannabis. ð
Just let me know which 3 or 4 options you'd like to move forward with.
__________________________________
Course 1
Kohlrabi & pork dumplings with brown butter, late-summer ponzu, grilled
pepper pickle
Shrimp & pork dumplings with maple-soy sauce, pickled ginger
Course 2
Slow-roasted squash with parmesan cream foam, candied spiced nuts, crispy
shallots
Smoked peppers filled with goat cream cheese, black walnuts, lilac garlic
vinaigrette
Grilled tomato filled with whipped herb ricotta, warm garden broth, crispy
onions
Fried pickles with fermented garlic aioli, chopped chives
Course 3
Aged grilled ribeye and butter-poached mini lobster roll with lemon chive
aioli, summer squash, zucchini, onion, rosemary-sherry sauce
Spice-rubbed filet and haddock with Ritz crumb, lemon butter sauce, grilled
tomatoes, warm summer broth, crispy potato pavÃ©
Course 4
Mocha chocolate mousse with seasonal fruit
Chocolate lava cake with seasonal fruit, gelato
Basque cheesecake with sweet fern crust, marinated berries
Peach custard tart with white tea gelato
Strawberry shortcake with candied lime balm
Chocolate and hazelnut bread pudding with chocolate ganache, raspberry

fixtures.body: Hi David, Now my turn to apologize for the delay - itâs been a wedding worldwind weekend! I hope youâre feeling better - we had Covid a few weeks ago! The menu looks amazing! We are heading to Naples on Thursday and canât wait for this dinner! Pat loved the surprise (I told him the night before our wedding)! Thanks again! fixtures.subject: Re: Private Chef Dinner - September
fixtures.body:
Haha you're good. Emailing is a lot sometimes! Huge congratulations on
getting married, you must be having a blast! I'll make sure that I only add
to it.
Thanks for the selections, we have everything we need. ð
 I will be providing my own cannabis for the event, which will be just one
serving for the night. If you're interested in having something that you
can take home that will eventually have multiple servings, feel free to
just provide me a gram of concentrate when I get there. The nearest
dispensary is called Blue Lobster, they are well informed + used to
providing the concentrates for my guests. They're very good friends of
mine, you'll be in great hands.
For example, when I do my cannabis dinners the THC serving size can be
anywhere from 1mg to 80mg. One and done. Or what most people opt in for;
you can give me concentrate which I then turn into a tincture and give back
to you. That tincture would be around 700mg total. So you get a lot more
bang for your buck, could be a fun little present for you guys to walk off
with! (I don't purchase the concentrate but I can prepare it for you)

The nearest dispensary is called Blue Lobster, they are well informed + used to providing the concentrates for my guests. They're very good friends of mine, you'll be in great hands. For example, when I do my cannabis dinners the THC serving size can be anywhere from 1mg to 80mg. One and done. Or what most people opt in for; you can give me concentrate which I then turn into a tincture and give back to you. That tincture would be around 700mg total. So you get a lot more bang for your buck, could be a fun little present for you guys to walk off with! (I don't purchase the concentrate but I can prepare it for you) On Tue, Sep 16, 2025, 7:08â¯PM Elisa Perel wrote: Hi David, Now my turn to apologize for the delay - itâs been a wedding worldwind weekend! fixtures.subject: Re: Private Chef Dinner - September
fixtures.body: Sorry for the confusion - I think we are all set with taking tincture home if thatâs OK! Do we still need to get anything at the dispensary? We can eat around 7, so weâll see you around 5! Do you need the address? Thanks! Elisa Sent from my iPhone On Sep 18, 2025, at 8:40â¯PM, David Ferragamo wrote: ï»¿ Awesome! I let them know you'll be stopping by. Just let me know when you would like to eat and I'll arrive 2 hours prior.ð On Thu, Sep 18, 2025, 6:44â¯PM Elisa Perel wrote: All sounds awesome - thank you! I think we are good on taking tincture home, but Iâll let you know if that changes. What time should we expect to see you on Saturday? We are really looking forward to this dinner! Elisa Sent from my iPhone On Sep 18, 2025, at 12:52â¯PM, David Ferragamo wrote: ï»¿ Haha you're good. Emailing is a lot sometimes! Huge congratulations on getting married, you must be having a blast! I'll make sure that I only add to it. Thanks for the selections, we have everything we need. ð I will be providing my own cannabis for the event, which will be just one serving for the night. If you're interested in having something that you can take home that will eventually have multiple servings, feel free to just provide me a gram of concentrate when I get there. The nearest dispensary is called Blue Lobster, they are well informed + used to providing the concentrates for my guests. They're very good friends of mine, you'll be in great hands. For example, when I do my cannabis dinners the THC serving size can be anywhere from 1mg to 80mg. One and done. Or what most people opt in for; you can give me concentrate which I then turn into a tincture and give back to you. That tincture would be around 700mg total. So you get a lot more bang for your buck, could be a fun little present for you guys to walk off with! (I don't purchase the concentrate but I can prepare it for you) On Tue, Sep 16, 2025, 7:08â¯PM Elisa Perel wrote: Hi David, Now my turn to apologize for the delay - itâs been a wedding worldwind weekend! I hope youâre feeling better - we had Covid a few weeks ago! The menu looks amazing! Iâd like to do: Pork dumplings Fried pickles Rib eye and lobster Mousse Thank you so much! Let me know if you have any other questions. We are heading to Naples on Thursday and canât wait for this dinner! Pat loved the surprise (I told him the night before our wedding)! Thanks again! Elisa Sent from my iPhone On Sep 14, 2025, at 1:32â¯PM, David Ferragamo wrote: ï»¿ Hey I'm really sorry for making you wait. I got caught with a horrible case of Covid and have been bedridden since we last spoke. Thank you very much for your patience. Below I have options I created for you, I will make sure there's a secrets candied bacon snack and cannabis. ð Just let me know which 3 or 4 options you'd like to move forward with. __________________________________ Course 1 Kohlrabi & pork dumplings with brown butter, late-summer ponzu, grilled pepper pickle Shrimp & pork dumplings with maple-soy sauce, pickled ginger Course 2 Slow-roasted squash with parmesan cream foam, candied spiced nuts, crispy shallots Smoked peppers filled with goat cream cheese, black walnuts, lilac garlic vinaigrette Grilled tomato filled with whipped herb ricotta, warm garden broth, crispy onions Fried pickles with fermented garlic aioli, chopped chives Course 3 Aged grilled ribeye and butter-poached mini lobster roll with lemon chive aioli, summer squash, zucchini, onion, rosemary-sherry sauce Spice-rubbed filet and haddock with Ritz crumb, lemon butter sauce, grilled tomatoes, warm summer broth, crispy potato pavÃ© Course 4 Mocha chocolate mousse with seasonal fruit Chocolate lava cake with seasonal fruit, gelato Basque cheesecake with sweet fern crust, marinated berries Peach custard tart with white tea gelato Strawberry shortcake with candied lime balm Chocolate and hazelnut bread pudding with chocolate ganache, raspberry On Thu, Sep 11, 2025, 9:19â¯AM Elisa Perel wrote: Thank you so much!! He was telling my brother about the cannabis infused private chef! Heâs going to be so excited! Thanks again, looking forward to sorting out the menu! Have a great weekend! Elisa Sent from my iPhone On Sep 11, 2025, at 7:09â¯AM, David Ferragamo wrote: ï»¿ Omgg hahah that's so funny. Classic move, he doesn't expect a thing! I'll make sure he has the best meal! I'm really excited for you guys! Next time we chat you'll be a married woman! ð On Thu, Sep 11, 2025, 6:54â¯AM Elisa Perel wrote: No worries at all! We are getting married on Saturday and Iâm going through my lists! He actually mentioned you last night - saying we should look into that private chef and I ignored it! Thank you again! Iâm really excited! Elisa Sent from my iPhone On Sep 10, 2025, at 9:54â¯PM, David Ferragamo wrote: ï»¿ Lol you're good! It's been a crazy month for me, you're totally warranted for the follow-up. Just give me a couple more days. I'm really sorry On Wed, Sep 10, 2025, 7:18â¯PM Elisa Perel wrote: Hi David, I hope all is well! I just wanted to check in - do you know when youâll have menu options available? No rush, just checking in! Thanks! Im ready to chat. ð 9789940570 On Thu, Aug 21, 2025, 12:02â¯PM David Ferragamo wrote: Works for me! ð On Wed, Aug 20, 2025, 10:06â¯AM Elisa Perel wrote: Hi Dave, Thank you! I am so excited! Can I call you at 5 on Thursday? Thanks! Elisa On Tuesday, August 19, 2025 at 07:47:05 PM EDT, David Ferragamo wrote: Thank you very much for your feedback. That makes my menu building a lot more helpful and personal. We can def get him some cannabis with your meal being effected, that's no issue. I'll make some really good bacon, you're talking to the right guy haha I've got everything else covered. I loved how specific you were, it lets me really hone in on what you'd like! Sorry for my response time. I'm around Thursday if you'd like to chat. ð My number is 978-994-0570 Also feel free to check out my Instagram, I'm always very active. I post my dinners and daily food stuff. It'll give you an idea of the service I provide. On Mon, Aug 18, 2025, 1:50â¯PM Elisa Perel wrote: Hello David, I hope you had a great weekend and enjoyed the beautiful weather! Did you have availability for a call this week? Thank you! Elisa On Friday, August 15, 2025 at 03:37:04 PM EDT, Elisa Perel wrote: Hello, Thank you for this information - super helpful and I am excited! My schedule this Monday, August 18th is pretty flexible - do you have a preferred time? I did think of one thing we both love - dumplings! And we would both love to watch you prepare them. For dessert, Pat loves mocha flavor and we love chocolate mousse with some funky fruit and/or candied bacon? He loves bacon! Everything sounds delicious! No salad for the app or veg as Pat hates it - I'm working on that one! As for mains, lobster, surf and turf would be great. He does not like salmon, scallops, duck or short rib. We love steak, squash or root veggies, potatoes...I think lobster/surf and turf would be really nice! We also loves haddock. Does that help at all? This is a first for me so guidance is appreciated. Would it be possible to make something for him with cannabis? Not for me, so I totally understand if that is not possible to do a dish separate. I look forward to connecting - let me know what time works best! Thanks! Elisa Perel 508-737-2766 On Friday, August 15, 2025 at 11:12:48 AM EDT, David Ferragamo wrote: That makes a lot of sense. I figured you were booking through one of those airbnb's because I remember reading that you said you would be in Naples ð but the wix form set Seabrook my apologies!!! I can send over the current growing summer menu that I've been adding too. Every menu I make is custom, so I just keep adding the summer dishes to it. Let me know if you find anything you like! I will make some more selections based on everything you said. The 20th sounds great to me! I can definitely hope on a phone call, perhaps one of these upcoming Mondays? I'm glad I could come be a part of it all. It sounds like a very fun memorable time! Does your husband have any favorites? Perhaps we can surprise them. Thank you! The summer options are below, I'll make more options based on your feedback from things you've always stated. ð§âð³ VEG / APP â¢ SautÃ©ed snap pea & broccolini salad with brown butter vinaigrette, manchego â¢ Grilled stone fruit, arugula, vegan ricotta, preserved pepper vinaigrette â¢ Summer salad with blueberry balsamic vinaigrette and fresh mozzarella â¢ Chopped beet, spinach & charred onion salad with black pepper blueberry vinaigrette, dried lime balm, goat cheese â¢ Tomato gazpacho with crispy shallot oil, basil, pruned blueberry capers â¢ Smoked peppers filled with goat cream cheese, black walnuts, lilac garlic vinaigrette â¢ Strawberry carpaccio with charred corn relish, goat cheese, herbs â¢ Tomato, arugula & herb salad with charred strawberry balsamic vinaigrette, mint, tarragon â¢ Melon & cucumber gazpacho with local feta â¢ Cucumber & pea salad with brown butter lemon vinaigrette, manchego â¢ Grilled Texas toast with whipped herb goat cheese, pepper jelly, raw zucchini salad â¢ Grilled & charred eggplant with pickled giardiniera, basil croutons, feta â¢ Fried pickles with fermented garlic aioli, chopped chives â¢ Bloominâ onion with horseradish aioli, sour cream chive seasoning MAIN â¢ Marinated grilled NY sirloin with smoked tomato butter, creamed spinach, crispy garlic â¢ BBQ steak tips with buttered asparagus, braised tomatoes, grilled potato salad â¢ Blackberry smoked & braised short rib with grilled vegetable salad, cornbread â¢ Chicken roulade stuffed with tomato, basil, asparagus, crispy potato â¢ Roasted chicken with herb jus, burnt onion, turnips, marinated cherry â¢ Crispy duck with peach mostarda, sautÃ©ed broccolini, burnt onion, herb broth â¢ Haddock with herb crumb, sautÃ©ed zucchini, pea shoots, basil butter â¢ Salmon with black pepper maple glaze, sautÃ©ed beans, herb rice â¢ Scallops pan-seared with tomato & spinach, cauliflower parm purÃ©e â¢ Butter-poached lobster roll with lemon chive aioli, hand-cut tossed fries, and house slaw DESSERT â¢ Basque cheesecake with sweet fern crust, marinated berries â¢ Peach custard tart with white tea gelato â¢ Peach buckwheat buckle cake with lilac icing, cheesecake gelato â¢ Vanilla custard with marinated stone fruit, candied black walnut â¢ Strawberry shortcake with candied lime balm â¢ Chocolate & hazelnut bread pudding with chocolate ganache, raspberry â¢ Chocoflan with chocolate chip gelato â¢ Chocolate lava cake with seasonal fruit, gelato -Chef Ferragamo On Fri, Aug 15, 2025, 8:46â¯AM Elisa Perel wrote: Hi David, Happy Friday! Thank you for the quick response! The dinner is actually in Naples, Maine - not New Hampshire - does that change anything with the cannabis dinner? We are staying at an Airbnb in Maine for the nights listed. The kitchen is gorgeous, I can send you the link if that is helpful. Would you have any sample menus by chance or be available for a phone call to discuss? I am trying to decide between 3 or 4 courses. We do not have any allergies or major dietary restrictions, we love lobster, mussels, steaks, root veggies, broccoli, broccoli rabe, broccolini... I love all veggies, my partner hates "salad" but I love it. Pat doesn't like oysters, clams or shrimp - I love them all. Can we plan on our last night, Sept 20th for the dinner? Let me know if you have a few minutes next week for a call and I can provide more detail. Thank you so much! We have never done anything like this before and this will be my surprise wedding gift for my partner. He has been talking about trying to do this since the Airbnb host shared your details and we started looking through your reviews. Thanks again! Elisa On Thursday, August 14, 2025 at 09:04:03 PM EDT, David Ferragamo wrote: Hi Elisa, Thank you for reaching out, I received the wix form as well. Congratulations on your upcoming mini-moon! I would love to surprise my finance with a really special dinner if you have availability while we are there. We will be staying in Naples Sept 18, 19, and 20th. Would you be available for a private dinner for two one of those nights? My partner would also love a meal cooked with cannabis if that is available for 1 and a regular meal for the me. I look forward to hearing from you! Thank you! Elisa Perel 08-737-2766 =

fixtures.subject: Possible Dinner in New Hampshire
fixtures.body:
Hi Df,
You were highly recommended by Lauren Boccelli as a possible hire for a
private dinner.
Our family will be staying in Alton, New Hampshire from Dec 26th til the
29th and would like to have a night whem dinner is extra special.
It will be roughly 22 people with 6 kids ranging from age 5 to 15 and the
rest are adults.
It will be at a home we arw renting for the dates above.
Menu would need to be gluten free and budget friendly.  We can do sit down
or buffett style.  The great thing is our family loves food and you can get
creative with the menu.  We would like to do this one of the nights we are
staying at the property.
Not sure if you are available and what is your minimum cost to do this
service.
My phone number is 774 526 0148 and looking forward to hopefully work with
you.
Have a wonderful day!
Virginia Hynes

fixtures.subject: In Airbnb Private Dinner in Naples, Maine? - This Friday?
fixtures.body:
Hello Chef,
My name is Mike McGovern, my wife and I will be visiting an Airbnb in
Naples, Maine this upcoming weekend and was wondering if you might be
available to do a private dinner in our Airbnb for us two on Friday night
in the 6pm range?  It's the Airbnb, located at 36 Brilliant Way, hosted by
Jon, Rose, and Courtney.  We got your name from them.  I know it's last
minute and on a busy hospitality night to boot, but figured it can't hurt
to check.
Please let me know.  Maybe see you Friday night.
Have a great week!
Mike

fixtures.subject: Custom Dinner Options – August 2 in Harrison
fixtures.body:
Hey Robert,
Thanks for reaching out, and I appreciate you being patient while I catch
up. Iâd love to cook for you during your trip to Harrison.
I attached a sample Summer 2025 menu to give you a feel for what Iâve been
serving lately. This is just a sample Iâve been offering since June. I
mainly make custom menus, but Iâm super flexible. If you see anything on
there that looks good, feel free to pick directly from it, or I can put
together completely new options based on what youâre into. Totally up to
you.
I saw the note about the onion. Weâll make sure to leave it out.
Hereâs the pricing for groups of three or more:
$155 per person for three courses
$185 per person for four courses
$215 per person for five courses
We also offer large tasting menus, up to 15 plus courses. This would have
different rates.
And just so you can see both formats, hereâs the couple pricing too:
$200 per person for three courses
$250 per person for four courses
$300 per person for five courses
We also offer large tasting menus, up to 15 plus courses. This would have
different rates.
I work closely with local farms, and I also forage and grow a lot of my own
ingredients. I try to keep the majority of each dinner farm to table
whenever possible, to represent the community and the season.
Let me know what kind of dishes youâre drawn to or if you want me to send
over a few custom options. Looking forward to hearing what sounds good.
Talk soon,
Chef Ferragamo
PF Private Chef

fixtures.subject: Re: 60th Birthday Dinner
fixtures.body:
Hey Jill,
I just finished up the November menu. I hope you enjoy it! Just pick 1 from
each, feel free to let me know of any changes you'd like to make.
1.
shaved kohlrabi + green apple salad w/ confit hazelnut, vermouth pickled
shallots, manchego cheese + sumac dill vinaigrette
warmed brie w/ greenhouse greens, confit potato + turnip, shallot,
pomegranate, walnut, birch syrup vinaigrette
roasted beet + turnip + rutabaga salad w/ citrus goat cheese, arugula,
pear, pecan crackers
2.
caramelized celeriac soup w/ thyme buttermilk, granny smith apple
compressed in herb vinegar, rye crouton
pumpkin soup, chamomile buttermilk, caramelized mushrooms + pumpkin seeds,
toasted pumpkin gut + seed oil
potato + leek soup w/ fermented garlic honey, poached egg + crunchy garlic
herbs
classic zuppa toscana + rosemary focaccia
3.
pan-seared duck breast + jus w/ quince jam, turnip puree, burnt onion,
seasonal green
braised beef w/ fondant potato, carrots, onions + snow pea greens
seafood au gratin w/ sauteed seasonal greens, roast pepper + peas
turkey or chicken breast filled w/ stuffing farce, gravy, cranberry sauce,
brussel sprout, green beans, carrot
4.
slow-roasted butternut custard w/ chai granola, preserved peaches, vanilla
bean ice cream + toasted pumpkin seed oil
mead poached + caramelized pears w/ vanilla anglaise, mead caramel, figs,
mint, candied orange peel
chocolate chip + wintergreen mousse tart w/ preserved raspberry
double chocolate chip brownie w/ preserved strawberry + black walnut ice
cream

fixtures.subject: Dinner Quote
fixtures.body:
I was given your info by my Airbnb host, Jon. I was reaching out to see if you had any availability to come cook at the Airbnb next Monday, Tuesday, or Wednesday. And if you are available, if you could give me an estimate for dinner for two.
Thanks,
Chase Garrett
Measurement Technician
[cid:image001.png@01DC36C7.26F89790]
350 W Mills Rd.
Breaux Bridge, LA 70517
Office # 225-675-5378
Mobile # 225-461-5120
Email: CJGarrett@eprod.com<mailto:CJGarrett@eprod.com>
________________________________
This message (including any attachments) is confidential and intended for a specific individual and purpose. If you are not the intended recipient, please notify the sender immediately and delete this message.

fixtures.subject: Re: Dinner Quote
fixtures.body:
Hey Chase,
Thanks for reaching out. Unfortunately I'm fully booked up on those dates.
I'm going to  reach out to my Wednesday client, who shouldn't have a
problem switching. I should have an answer for you by the morning, they're
super flexible. That would be the only day I'm available, I hope that
works!
Below is my pricing and every menu is custom. All I ask is that you provide
me any strong dislikes/favorites + allergies. Then I can start building out
a menu towards your style. Please feel free to leave any additional
information such as the occasion, preferences, etc.
Couples (Two Guests)
3 Courses - $200/person ($400 total)
4 Courses - $250/person ($500 total)
5 Courses - $300/person ($600 total) Large Tasting Menu (8-15+ courses) -
Custom rates
- Chef Ferragamo

fixtures.subject: Re: [EXTERNAL] Re: Dinner Quote
fixtures.body:
Here is what she sent me.
We’re so excited to have you cook for us during our honeymoon and wanted to share a few preferences to help guide the menu planning.
We don’t have any dietary restrictions or food allergies. A few favorites of ours are tuna, fresh local seafood, and dishes that incorporate fresh herbs and spice - we both love bold, flavorful food.
There are just a couple of things we’re not big fans of: squid or octopus, and raspberries, or cherries.
The overall vibe we’re hoping for is a cozy, romantic date night. We’ll also be bringing a selection of wines from our recent trip to Napa Valley, and would love for the dishes to pair nicely with those (we can send you a list, we will have a vintahe chardonnay and likely a Cabernet of red blend).
We’re both foodies and love trying new things, so please feel free to get creative with the menu. If possible, we’d appreciate seeing a draft of the menu in advance so we can review it and request any small adjustments if needed.
Thank you so much — we’re really looking forward to the experience!
Warmly,
Kay & Chase
Thanks,
Chase Garrett
Enterprise Products
Measurement Technician
47433 Texaco Rd.
Sorrento, LA 70778
Work: 225-461-5120
Home: 985-519-1303
CJGarrett@eprod.com
Sent from my iPhone

Hey, I just wrapped up the menu. Thanks for being patient! Let me know
which 5 you both would enjoy going forward with. I hope you enjoy the
selections. ð
veg
â¢ roasted squash, charred onion and garlic goat cheese, toasted crumble of
fried delicata squash, shallot, toasted almonds, and marigold petals.
â¢ slow-roasted beets, sumac blackberry vinaigrette, marigolds, toasted
pumpkin, goat cheese, candied walnuts
â¢ braised carrots, whipped garlic ricotta, carrot butter, tossed crumb of
brown bread, almonds, herbs, nasturtium flowers+ bella mushrooms
â¢ charred corn, spinach + brined pepper w/ sweet corn puree, parmesan foam
+ crispy garlic
â¢ butternut soup w/ cinnamon cream, delicate chips + brown sage butter
â¢ fried cheese curd + potato balls, w/ leek aoili,
seafood
â¢ spiced tuna, new england ponzu, smoked pumpkin + bronze fennel
â¢ pan-seared seabass, celeriac puree, roasted white acorn squash, basil +
dandelion sauce
â¢ baked halibut+herb crumb, potatoes au gratin, sauted spinach
â¢ sautÃ©ed scallops with crispy pork belly, braised cabbage, apple/pork
broth + brown sage butter
â¢ lobster thermador, lemon butter, herb potatoes + peas
â¢ crispy salmon w/ goat cheese risotto, buttered squash + bean, maple sage
sauce
meat
â¢ braised shirt rib + mushroom ragu, confit garlic manchego foam, crispy
leeks
â¢ crispy pork belly + apple/pear sauce, fried potato bread cake, dandelion
â¢ spiced ribeye, creamy potato + leek, brined pepper + sherry sauce
â¢ crispy duck breast, pear/apple jam, carmelized turnips, cauliflower +
brown butter duck jus
â¢ extra crispy chicken slider, Maine relish + garlic honey w/ curly fry +
fancy sauce
dessert
â¢ chocolate lava, concord grape sorbet, marinated plums+magnolia
â¢ pumpkin cheesecake, chocolate crust, pumpkin spice latte gelato
â¢ pear crisp, cinnamon gelato, salted caramel
â¢ chocoflan, blueberry cookies+cream w/ chocolate shell
â¢ pumpkin cinnamon roll w/ vanilla gelato + candied pumpkin

fixtures.subject: Re: [EXTERNAL] Re: Dinner Quote
fixtures.body:
After how difficult that was to decide, here’s what we have decided on!
1. Charred corn, spinach + brine pepper with sweet corn puree, Parmesan foam + crispy garlic
2. Spiced tuna, New England ponzu, smoked pumpkin + bronze fennel
3. Crispy duck breast, pear/apple jam, caramelized turnips, cauliflower + brown butter duck jus
4. Chocolate lava, Concord grape sorbet, marinated plums + magnolia
These are the four we selected, one from each category. Were we supposed to pick two of one of these options to make 5 courses?
Thanks,
Chase Garrett
Enterprise Products
Measurement Technician
47433 Texaco Rd.
Sorrento, LA 70778
Work: 225-461-5120
Home: 985-519-1303
CJGarrett@eprod.com
Sent from my iPhone

• butternut soup w/ cinnamon cream, delicate chips + brown sage butter
• fried cheese curd + potato balls, w/ leek aoili,
seafood
• spiced tuna, new england ponzu, smoked pumpkin + bronze fennel
• pan-seared seabass, celeriac puree, roasted white acorn squash, basil + dandelion sauce
• baked halibut+herb crumb, potatoes au gratin, sauted spinach
• sautéed scallops with crispy pork belly, braised cabbage, apple/pork broth + brown sage butter
• lobster thermador, lemon butter, herb potatoes + peas
• crispy salmon w/ goat cheese risotto, buttered squash + bean, maple sage sauce
meat
• braised shirt rib + mushroom ragu, confit garlic manchego foam, crispy leeks
• crispy pork belly + apple/pear sauce, fried potato bread cake, dandelion
• spiced ribeye, creamy potato + leek, brined pepper + sherry sauce
• crispy duck breast, pear/apple jam, carmelized turnips, cauliflower + brown butter duck jus
• extra crispy chicken slider, Maine relish + garlic honey w/ curly fry + fancy sauce

• chocolate lava, concord grape sorbet, marinated plums+magnolia
