On Sun, Oct 12, 2025, 12:36 PM David Ferragamo <dfprivatechef@gmail.com<mailto:dfprivatechef@gmail.com>> wrote:
Hey thank you so much for all this information. I've been very busy but plan to get your the menu options by tomorrow. 🧑‍🍳
On Tue, Oct 7, 2025, 4:41 PM Garrett, Chase <CJGarrett@eprod.com<mailto:CJGarrett@eprod.com>> wrote:
Here is what she sent me.
We’re so excited to have you cook for us during our honeymoon and wanted to share a few preferences to help guide the menu planning.
We don’t have any dietary restrictions or food allergies. A few favorites of ours are tuna, fresh local seafood, and dishes that incorporate fresh herbs and spice - we both love bold, flavorful food.
There are just a couple of things we’re not big fans of: squid or octopus, and raspberries, or cherries.
The overall vibe we’re hoping for is a cozy, romantic date night. We’ll also be bringing a selection of wines from our recent trip to Napa Valley, and would love for the dishes to pair nicely with those (we can send you a list, we will have a vintahe chardonnay and likely a Cabernet of red blend).
We’re both foodies and love trying new things, so please feel free to get creative with the menu. If possible, we’d appreciate seeing a draft of the menu in advance so we can review it and request any small adjustments if needed.
Thank you so much — we’re really looking forward to the experience!
Warmly,
Kay & Chase
Thanks,
Chase Garrett
Enterprise Products
Measurement Technician
47433 Texaco Rd.
Sorrento, LA 70778
Work: 225-461-5120
Home: 985-519-1303
CJGarrett@eprod.com<mailto:CJGarrett@eprod.com>
Sent from my iPhone

Thank you for the selections! Yes just pick one more to make it a five
course, anything you'd like. The first squash dish is excellent with these
picks, or even the pumpkin roll for a second dessert would be great as
well. Totally up to you! ð§âð³

After how difficult that was to decide, here’s what we have decided on!
1. Charred corn, spinach + brine pepper with sweet corn puree, Parmesan foam + crispy garlic
2. Spiced tuna, New England ponzu, smoked pumpkin + bronze fennel
3. Crispy duck breast, pear/apple jam, caramelized turnips, cauliflower + brown butter duck jus
4. Chocolate lava, Concord grape sorbet, marinated plums + magnolia
These are the four we selected, one from each category. Were we supposed to pick two of one of these options to make 5 courses?
Thanks,
Chase Garrett

Hey, I just wrapped up the menu. Thanks for being patient! Let me know which 5 you both would enjoy going forward with. I hope you enjoy the selections. 😊
veg
• roasted squash, charred onion and garlic goat cheese, toasted crumble of fried delicata squash, shallot, toasted almonds, and marigold petals.
• slow-roasted beets, sumac blackberry vinaigrette, marigolds, toasted pumpkin, goat cheese, candied walnuts
• braised carrots, whipped garlic ricotta, carrot butter, tossed crumb of brown bread, almonds, herbs, nasturtium flowers+ bella mushrooms
• charred corn, spinach + brined pepper w/ sweet corn puree, parmesan foam + crispy garlic
• butternut soup w/ cinnamon cream, delicate chips + brown sage butter
• fried cheese curd + potato balls, w/ leek aoili,
seafood
• spiced tuna, new england ponzu, smoked pumpkin + bronze fennel
• pan-seared seabass, celeriac puree, roasted white acorn squash, basil + dandelion sauce
• baked halibut+herb crumb, potatoes au gratin, sauted spinach
• sautéed scallops with crispy pork belly, braised cabbage, apple/pork broth + brown sage butter
• lobster thermador, lemon butter, herb potatoes + peas
• crispy salmon w/ goat cheese risotto, buttered squash + bean, maple sage sauce
meat
• braised shirt rib + mushroom ragu, confit garlic manchego foam, crispy leeks
• crispy pork belly + apple/pear sauce, fried potato bread cake, dandelion
• spiced ribeye, creamy potato + leek, brined pepper + sherry sauce
• crispy duck breast, pear/apple jam, carmelized turnips, cauliflower + brown butter duck jus
• extra crispy chicken slider, Maine relish + garlic honey w/ curly fry + fancy sauce
dessert
• chocolate lava, concord grape sorbet, marinated plums+magnolia
• pumpkin cheesecake, chocolate crust, pumpkin spice latte gelato
• pear crisp, cinnamon gelato, salted caramel
• chocoflan, blueberry cookies+cream w/ chocolate shell
• pumpkin cinnamon roll w/ vanilla gelato + candied pumpkin

Here is what she sent me.
We’re so excited to have you cook for us during our honeymoon and wanted to share a few preferences to help guide the menu planning.
We don’t have any dietary restrictions or food allergies. A few favorites of ours are tuna, fresh local seafood, and dishes that incorporate fresh herbs and spice - we both love bold, flavorful food.
There are just a couple of things we’re not big fans of: squid or octopus, and raspberries, or cherries.
The overall vibe we’re hoping for is a cozy, romantic date night. We’ll also be bringing a selection of wines from our recent trip to Napa Valley, and would love for the dishes to pair nicely with those (we can send you a list, we will have a vintahe chardonnay and likely a Cabernet of red blend).
We’re both foodies and love trying new things, so please feel free to get creative with the menu. If possible, we’d appreciate seeing a draft of the menu in advance so we can review it and request any small adjustments if needed.
Thank you so much — we’re really looking forward to the experience!
Warmly,
Kay & Chase
Thanks,
Chase Garrett

veg

• braised carrots, whipped garlic ricotta, carrot butter, tossed crumb of brown bread, almonds, herbs, nasturtium flowers+ bella mushrooms
• charred corn, spinach + brined pepper w/ sweet corn puree, parmesan foam + crispy garlic
• butternut soup w/ cinnamon cream, delicate chips + brown sage butter
• fried cheese curd + potato balls, w/ leek aoili,
seafood
• spiced tuna, new england ponzu, smoked pumpkin + bronze fennel
• pan-seared seabass, celeriac puree, roasted white acorn squash, basil + dandelion sauce
• baked halibut+herb crumb, potatoes au gratin, sauted spinach
• sautéed scallops with crispy pork belly, braised cabbage, apple/pork broth + brown sage butter
• lobster thermador, lemon butter, herb potatoes + peas
• crispy salmon w/ goat cheese risotto, buttered squash + bean, maple sage sauce
meat
• braised shirt rib + mushroom ragu, confit garlic manchego foam, crispy leeks
• crispy pork belly + apple/pear sauce, fried potato bread cake, dandelion
• spiced ribeye, creamy potato + leek, brined pepper + sherry sauce
• crispy duck breast, pear/apple jam, carmelized turnips, cauliflower + brown butter duck jus
• extra crispy chicken slider, Maine relish + garlic honey w/ curly fry + fancy sauce
dessert
• chocolate lava, concord grape sorbet, marinated plums+magnolia
• pumpkin cheesecake, chocolate crust, pumpkin spice latte gelato
• pear crisp, cinnamon gelato, salted caramel
• chocoflan, blueberry cookies+cream w/ chocolate shell
• pumpkin cinnamon roll w/ vanilla gelato + candied pumpkin

Here is what she sent me.
We’re so excited to have you cook for us during our honeymoon and wanted to share a few preferences to help guide the menu planning.
We don’t have any dietary restrictions or food allergies. A few favorites of ours are tuna, fresh local seafood, and dishes that incorporate fresh herbs and spice - we both love bold, flavorful food.
There are just a couple of things we’re not big fans of: squid or octopus, and raspberries, or cherries.
The overall vibe we’re hoping for is a cozy, romantic date night. We’ll also be bringing a selection of wines from our recent trip to Napa Valley, and would love for the dishes to pair nicely with those (we can send you a list, we will have a vintahe chardonnay and likely a Cabernet of red blend).
We’re both foodies and love trying new things, so please feel free to get creative with the menu. If possible, we’d appreciate seeing a draft of the menu in advance so we can review it and request any small adjustments if needed.
Thank you so much — we’re really looking forward to the experience!
Warmly,
Kay & Chase
Thanks,

fixtures.subject: Re: [EXTERNAL] Re: Dinner Quote
fixtures.body:
Perfect! We were looking to be served around 6:00 PM. We will have the kitchen cleaned and I can move the car out of the way so you can get in and out easily. Just a heads up in case you haven’t been here before, there is no dining table and everything will need to be served on the island counter. We look forward to the experience with you!
Chase Garrett
Enterprise Products
Measurement Technician
47433 Texaco Rd.
Sorrento, LA 70778
Work: 225-461-5120
Home: 985-519-1303
CJGarrett@eprod.com

Hey, I just wrapped up the menu. Thanks for being patient! Let me know which 5 you both would enjoy going forward with. I hope you enjoy the selections. 😊
veg
• roasted squash, charred onion and garlic goat cheese, toasted crumble of fried delicata squash, shallot, toasted almonds, and marigold petals.
• slow-roasted beets, sumac blackberry vinaigrette, marigolds, toasted pumpkin, goat cheese, candied walnuts
• braised carrots, whipped garlic ricotta, carrot butter, tossed crumb of brown bread, almonds, herbs, nasturtium flowers+ bella mushrooms
• charred corn, spinach + brined pepper w/ sweet corn puree, parmesan foam + crispy garlic
• butternut soup w/ cinnamon cream, delicate chips + brown sage butter
• fried cheese curd + potato balls, w/ leek aoili,
seafood
• spiced tuna, new england ponzu, smoked pumpkin + bronze fennel
• pan-seared seabass, celeriac puree, roasted white acorn squash, basil + dandelion sauce
• baked halibut+herb crumb, potatoes au gratin, sauted spinach
• sautéed scallops with crispy pork belly, braised cabbage, apple/pork broth + brown sage butter
• lobster thermador, lemon butter, herb potatoes + peas
• crispy salmon w/ goat cheese risotto, buttered squash + bean, maple sage sauce
meat
• braised shirt rib + mushroom ragu, confit garlic manchego foam, crispy leeks
• crispy pork belly + apple/pear sauce, fried potato bread cake, dandelion
• spiced ribeye, creamy potato + leek, brined pepper + sherry sauce
• crispy duck breast, pear/apple jam, carmelized turnips, cauliflower + brown butter duck jus
• extra crispy chicken slider, Maine relish + garlic honey w/ curly fry + fancy sauce
dessert
• chocolate lava, concord grape sorbet, marinated plums+magnolia
• pumpkin cheesecake, chocolate crust, pumpkin spice latte gelato
• pear crisp, cinnamon gelato, salted caramel
• chocoflan, blueberry cookies+cream w/ chocolate shell
• pumpkin cinnamon roll w/ vanilla gelato + candied pumpkin

Here is what she sent me.
We’re so excited to have you cook for us during our honeymoon and wanted to share a few preferences to help guide the menu planning.
We don’t have any dietary restrictions or food allergies. A few favorites of ours are tuna, fresh local seafood, and dishes that incorporate fresh herbs and spice - we both love bold, flavorful food.
There are just a couple of things we’re not big fans of: squid or octopus, and raspberries, or cherries.
The overall vibe we’re hoping for is a cozy, romantic date night. We’ll also be bringing a selection of wines from our recent trip to Napa Valley, and would love for the dishes to pair nicely with those (we can send you a list, we will have a vintahe chardonnay and likely a Cabernet of red blend).
We’re both foodies and love trying new things, so please feel free to get creative with the menu. If possible, we’d appreciate seeing a draft of the menu in advance so we can review it and request any small adjustments if needed.
Thank you so much — we’re really looking forward to the experience!
Warmly,
Kay & Chase
Thanks,
Chase Garrett

fixtures.subject: Re: [EXTERNAL] Re: Dinner Quote
fixtures.body:
Hey! Thank you for the information, I look at this location all the time so
you'll be in a good hands.
It looks like I'm running a bit late today, Ill be arriving close to 5. I
hope a serving time between 630-7 is okay. My apologies!
On Tue, Oct 14, 2025, 9:08â¯PM Garrett, Chase <CJGarrett@eprod.com> wrote:
> Perfect! We were looking to be served around 6:00 PM. We will have the
> kitchen cleaned and I can move the car out of the way so you can get in and
> out easily. Just a heads up in case you havenât been here before, there is
> no dining table and everything will need to be served on the island
> counter. We look forward to the experience with you!
>
> Chase Garrett
> Enterprise Products
> Measurement Technician
> 47433 Texaco Rd.
> Sorrento, LA 70778
> Work: 225-461-5120

> Here is what she sent me.
>
>
> Weâre so excited to have you cook for us during our honeymoon and wanted
> to share a few preferences to help guide the menu planning.
>
>
> We donât have any dietary restrictions or food allergies. A few favorites
> of ours are tuna, fresh local seafood, and dishes that incorporate fresh
> herbs and spice - we both love bold, flavorful food.
>
>
> There are just a couple of things weâre not big fans of: squid or octopus,
> and raspberries, or cherries.
>
>
> The overall vibe weâre hoping for is a cozy, romantic date night. Weâll
> also be bringing a selection of wines from our recent trip to Napa Valley,
> and would love for the dishes to pair nicely with those (we can send you a
> list, we will have a vintahe chardonnay and likely a Cabernet of red blend).
>
>
> Weâre both foodies and love trying new things, so please feel free to get
> creative with the menu. If possible, weâd appreciate seeing a draft of the
> menu in advance so we can review it and request any small adjustments if
> needed.
>
>
> Thank you so much â weâre really looking forward to the experience!
>
>
> Warmly,
>
> Kay & Chase

fixtures.subject: Dinner Follow-up
fixtures.body:
Hey Lindsey,
Great speaking with you on Instagram, I'm looking forward to creating your
dinner. Here is the link to the Q + A. https://forms.gle/yqBkxBVp3D2ugtxq6
*Rates per person*
Normal | $115/4course, $150/8course, $175/12course
Cannabis | $200/4course, $250/8course, $275/12course [Follow-up Required]
Guest count: min 5, max 20
*The party size must be confirmed up to 7 days before the event takes place.
*$20 up-charge per person for groups smaller than 5
*Day of Event*
We are a small team consisting of 1-3 chefs. Our team must have time to
arrive 3-4hrs early prior to dining. Upon arrival our crew will spend a
short time unloading equipment into your kitchen. We ask that all
countertop appliances must be removed to give us a full range of counter
space. It is appreciated that you provide a clean space for us to cook, in
which we will return the same. Thank you for your cooperation.
- Chef Ferragamo

fixtures.subject: Re: Dinner Follow-up
fixtures.body:
Hi David,
I just filled out the questionnaire, and meant to add in a note on the
protein preference.  I'm not sure if this is possible, please tell me if
it's not (and no worries), but would (2) of the guests be able to do Bison
and (2) Beef - simply because of a preference/taste thing?  We are not a
picky group and excited to eat whatever you put in front of us LOL so just
let me know.
Best
Lindsey

fixtures.subject: Re: Dinner Follow-up
fixtures.body:
Hey Lindsey,
Thank you for your response on the Q+A. The bison vs beef is no issue! I
have a few menu options for you to choose from.
Please just pick (1) from each category. Feel free to let us know if you'd
like to make any changes.
1st| Squash Soup, Brown Butter Sumac, Apple, Puffed Black Rice
1st| Golden Beet Salad, Fermented Husk cherry Vinaigrette, Leafy Greens,
Spiced Squash Seeds, Goat Cheese
1st| Mushroom + Onion Soup, Savory Carrot Toast w/ Smoked Charred Leek
Butter
1st| Squash Salad, Fermented Pear Vinaigrette, Spiced Squash Seed + Pecan
Brittle, Brie
2nd| Chicken Leek Sausage, Pumpkin Gnocchi, Leek Pinot Cream, Sage,
Pecorino
2nd| Chicken Thighs compressed w/ Marinated Apple, Roasted Peppers, Squash,
Chicken Apple Cider Jus
3rd| Bison or Beef Sirloin, Turnip, Cabbage, Mushrooms, Nasturtium
Fermented Garlic Honey, Pickled Pear
3rd| Bison or Beef Sirloin + Braised Shredded, Cabbage, Squash, Vermouth
Rosemary Demi
3rd| Bison or Beef Sirloin, Savory Carrot Bread, Confit Carrot, Leeks, Sour
Cream
4th| Apple, Black Walnut, Vanilla Chai
4th| Beet, Raspberry, Pumpernickel, Dark Chocolate
4th| Pumpkin, Salted Caramel, Cold Brew, Dark Chocolate
We are currently only booking for Saturday + Sunday, starting November 5th.
Please let us know which dates you had in mind.
Thank you for your patience!
- Chef Ferragamo

fixtures.subject: Green Geans LLC: Next Steps for Your Freeze-Drying Inquiry
fixtures.body:
Hi David!
Green Geans LLC specializes in freeze-drying, with extensive experience in processing both meals and individual ingredients. Weve worked with a wide variety of products, including fresh and frozen produce, herbs, shellfish (such as green crab and oysters), and foraged foods like spruce tips and flowers. However, its important to note that some items do not freeze-dry well, including large quantities of butter, oil, and honey, as well as fatty cuts of meat like brisket.
Pricing is made with incoming wet weight of $x per wet pounds. Estimate range from $8/ lbs. - $18/ lbs. We range this based on the services that are needed, this is why we want to get a full understanding of your vision.
Attached are a few pages related to the inquiry. Once they are filled out to reflect your goals and preferences, Ill have a better idea of how to approach packaging with you. Im also curious if youre looking for bulk orders or specific sizing that fits your recipes.
Feel free to reach out if you have any questions. Looking forward to hearing from you!
Cheerio,
-Gina M.
CEO of GREEN GEANS LLC
FREEZE-DRYING SERVICES
[https://ci3.googleusercontent.com/mail-sig/AIorK4wxH7iOKQkfrRnz440Rei6bLHszH22MtSzBlarb1ikbpXwVzJOscukIQ1vSP7xB57lDgkCgJCU]
A: 90 BRIDGE ST. STE 255 WESTBROOK, ME 04092
P: 207-413-7988  E: greengeans207@gmail.com<mailto:greengeans207@gmail.com>
W: www.greengeans.com<http://www.greengeans.com/>  IG: @greengeans<https://www.instagram.com/greengeans/?hl=en>

fixtures.subject: Autumn Dinner
fixtures.body:
Hi S+P,
Thank you for your patience, I normally donât take this long. Iâve been
working through a few earlier submissions, and Iâm pleased to say that your
menu was next in the queue. Iâve now had the chance to focus on it and put
together a tailored selection of options for your event.
Please take a look at the attached menu and let me know your final choices
or if youâd like any adjustments. Iâm happy to make any tweaks to ensure
your event is exactly how you envision it.
For the cannabis-infused option, please present us 1g of cannabis
concentrate upon arrival. I will convert the item and make it available for
consumption while we prep. In return, we will make a tincture that is used
during and left with you after our departure.
I highly recommend "rosin." Check out this dispensary nearest to you.
https://greenport.wm.store/?filter%5BanyClientCategories%5D%5B%5D=rosin-concentrates-category-pages
Price: $160 per person
Event Details:
Date: Oct 18
Location: 67 Main Street, Newcastle, ME
Guest Count: 8
Menu: Vegan Autumn Harvest
Arrival Time: (2.5 hours prior to serving)
Serving Time:
Allergies:
Looking forward to hearing from you soon!
-- Choose 4 Items --
1. Rutabaga, apple, potato terrine w/ cashew dill sauce + yarrow shrub
2. Melon carpaccio w/ celery, fennel, coriander vinaigrette, + pili nut
3. Sweet potato + fennel soup w/ caramelized fennel, pickled peppers +
almond cream
4. Fried mushroom + squash fire cider, elderberry powder
5. Braised eggplant + mushroom w/ autumn veg demi-glace, spiced nuts,
crunchy leeks
6. Cabbage filled w/ celeriac root puree, crispy eggplant + herb sourdough
crumb
Desserts:
1. Pears poached in maple syrup, birch w/ candied black walnut ice cream +
chai cake
2. Pumpkin pudding w/ macadamia sweet cream, caramel kettle corn +
chocolate mousse
3. Concord grape + chocolate tart w/ vanilla sorbet
Best regards,
David Ferragamo
PF Private Chef

October or early November? We are fairly flexible but I know that that
weekend of October 18 is not going to work.   it also doesnât need to be a
weekend for us if that helps you.
Thanks!!
Shelby

fixtures.subject: Private Dinner - August
fixtures.body:
Hey Dana,
Thank you for you patience in my response, as I've been in and out of the
kitchen this past week! I'm thrilled that you're interested in having me as
your private chef. I know we did already have some of the information down.
To make sure everything is perfect, could you please fix/fill out the
following. Will this information, I will follow up with a menu and price
options. Thank you!
   1.
   *Host:*
   - [Dana]
   2.
   *Date:*
   - [Aug 9,10 or 11]
   3.
   *Location:*
   - [Event Location]
   4.
   *Guest Count:*
   - [3-4]
      5.
   *Arrival + Serving Time:*
   *I will arrive 2 hours before dining begins.
      - [When should I arrive?]
      6.
   *Allergies:*
   - [Any allergies I should be aware of?]
   7.
   *Dietary Restrictions:*
   - [Any dietary restrictions?]
   8.
   *Menu Details:*
   *I will craft a seasonal, progressive New England-style menu.
   - Do you have any suggestions or specific ingredients you'd like to
      include?
      - Any dislikes or food to avoid?
   9.
   *Special Requests:*
   - [Anything special you'd like to include?]
   10.
   *Event Theme/Occasion:*
   - [Any theme or special occasion details?]
   11.
   *Feedback/Notes:*
   - [Anything else I should know?]
   12. *How did you hear about about services? *(Optional)
*Cannabis Consumption Questionnaire:*
To help tailor the experience to your preferences, please answer the
following questions:
-
*1. Is there a recreational cannabis store accessible in your area?*
[ ] Yes
[ ] No
-
*2. Have you or your guests consumed cannabis before?*
[ ] Yes
[ ] No
-
*3. How frequently do you or your guests consume cannabis?*
[ ] Rarely
[ ] Occasionally
[ ] Frequently
-
*4. What is your preferred method of consumption?*
[ ] Edibles
[ ] Smoking
[ ] Vaping
[ ] Other: [Please specify]
-
*5. Do you know you preferred dose?*
[ ]Yes
[ ] No
[ ] I don't know.
Just fill in the details and send it back to me. I'm excited to create a
gourmet dining experience that will make this event unforgettable!
Looking forward to hearing from you soon!
Be Regards,
Chef Ferragamo

fixtures.subject: Re: Private Dinner - August
fixtures.body:
Thank you for your patience as I craft your mother's birthday menu! Here
are some options I have created for you. I hope you enjoy them! I'm very
flexible, so don't hesitate to ask for any changes. ð
*Please choose one option for each course:*
*1.*
   - Chopped beet, spinach, and charred onion salad with fermented
   blueberry vinaigrette, lime balm, and goat cheese
   - Tomato and arugula salad with charred strawberry balsamic vinaigrette,
   mint, and tarragon
   - Confit potato, kohlrabi, greens, and radish salad with daylily
   vinaigrette
*2.*
   - Watermelon gazpacho with pickled peppers, grilled vegetables, and
   fresh anise
   - Cucumber gazpacho poured beside a salad of marinated cucumber,
   dill-cured salmon, tomato, and feta cheese
   - Corn gazpacho with scallop crudo, tomato jam, smoked buttermilk, and
   basil
*3.*
   - Crispy chicken with a farce of mushroom and ramp, served beside
   rutabaga purÃ©e. Crispy chicken with chicken consommÃ© French-pressed with
   herbs and flowers (table side)
   - Poached lobster with zucchini breadcrumbs, zucchini, asparagus, and
   beurre montÃ©
   - Rack of lamb with dandelion and black walnut herb crust, sautÃ©ed
   vegetables, and mint
   - Crispy duck breast dusted with lilac salt blend, with pear jam,
   celeriac purÃ©e, confit garlic butter kale, and crispy potato
*4.*
   - CrÃ¨me brÃ»lÃ©e tart with blueberry and young mint
   - Melon buckle cake with marinated melon, whipped cream, and mint
   - Chocolate lava cake with strawberry and gelato
   - Cornbread pudding with marinated blueberry and gelato
*Cannabis Addition:*
DF Private Chef cannot supply cannabis, but we are experts in preparing it
for consumption.
Please purchase 1g of concentrate from your nearest dispensary.
   - If you are visiting ATG in Salisbury, please visit ATG Salisbury
   <https://dutchie.com/embedded-menu/alternative-therapies-group-salisbury/products/concentrates>
   .
   - If you'll be in Ogunquit, Silver Therapeutics has a great
selection. Silver
   Therapeutics Berwick
   <https://www.leafly.com/dispensary-info/silver-therapeutics---berwick/menu?product_category%5B%5D=Concentrate&product_category%5B%5D=Other>
   .
When your chef arrives, please make the concentrate available. In return,
we will convert it into an oil-based tincture ready for consumption.
We advise purchasing only concentrate, as it offers a direct conversion for
making edibles. Flower wouldnât provide definite results in time.
If the store you visit has tinctures, we strongly advise not buying them.
We will make a much stronger tincture for the same price! Most store-bought
tinctures don't reach high strength, maxing at 300mg. Any concentrate you
buy will be 70-85% per gram, which can be converted into a 700-850mg
tincture. This gives us full control of the product and leaves you with a
whole tincture when we leave.

fixtures.subject: Re: Private Dinner - August
fixtures.body:
Hi!!
Here are the Choices/ Feedbackâ¦
Wow! Everything sounds delicious, there are a couple things I am not sure
she enjoys..
1.
Chopped beet, spinach, and charred onion salad with fermented blueberry
vinaigrette, lime balm, and goat cheeseâ¦***Not sure if she would enjoy the
charred onion.
Tomato and arugula salad with charred strawberry balsamic vinaigrette,
mint, and tarragonâ¦****Unsure if she likes tarragon. Otherwise the salads
are up her alley.
2. ****I do not think she is a huge fan of gazpacho. I know she prefers
scallops cooked, not raw. She enjoys either grilled (almost burnt salmon).
Not sure if youâd be able to do a tuna crudo, sashimi or tartar? Other apps
she orders a lot is crab cakes and grilled shrimp or shrimp cocktail.
3.  ***She does not like lamb or kale. Loves duck so if that could be made
different probably that if not I would say the poached lobster. She prefers
claw over tail.
   - Poached lobster with zucchini breadcrumbs, zucchini, asparagus, and
   beurre montÃ©
   - Crispy duck breast dusted with lilac salt blend, with pear jam,
   celeriac purÃ©e, confit garlic butter kale, and crispy potato
4. Chocolate lava cake with strawberry and gelato** sounds perfect!
Look forward to hearing from you!
Warmly,
Dana Epstein

fixtures.subject: Re: Private Dinner - August
fixtures.body:
Let me know what you think about these options!
1. cooked, raw kohlrabi salad + grilled tomato salad dressed w/ herb
toasted black walnut finished with a creamy parmesan sauce
or
mixed green salad w/ blueberry vinaigrette, croutons + mozzarella
2. tuna crudo w/ cucumber + radish dressed purple pepper + basil
vinaigrette
or

>>>>> Thank you for your patience as I craft your mother's birthday menu!
>>>>> Here are some options I have created for you. I hope you enjoy them! I'm
>>>>> very flexible, so don't hesitate to ask for any changes. ð
>>>>>

>>>>> *1.*
>>>>>
>>>>>    - Chopped beet, spinach, and charred onion salad with fermented
>>>>>    blueberry vinaigrette, lime balm, and goat cheese
>>>>>    - Tomato and arugula salad with charred strawberry balsamic
>>>>>    vinaigrette, mint, and tarragon
>>>>>    - Confit potato, kohlrabi, greens, and radish salad with daylily
>>>>>    vinaigrette
>>>>>
>>>>> *2.*
>>>>>
>>>>>    - Watermelon gazpacho with pickled peppers, grilled vegetables,
>>>>>    and fresh anise
>>>>>    - Cucumber gazpacho poured beside a salad of marinated cucumber,
>>>>>    dill-cured salmon, tomato, and feta cheese
>>>>>    - Corn gazpacho with scallop crudo, tomato jam, smoked buttermilk,
>>>>>    and basil
>>>>>
>>>>> *3.*
>>>>>
>>>>>    - Crispy chicken with a farce of mushroom and ramp, served beside
>>>>>    rutabaga purÃ©e. Crispy chicken with chicken consommÃ© French-pressed with
>>>>>    herbs and flowers (table side)
>>>>>    - Poached lobster with zucchini breadcrumbs, zucchini, asparagus,
>>>>>    and beurre montÃ©
>>>>>    - Rack of lamb with dandelion and black walnut herb crust, sautÃ©ed
>>>>>    vegetables, and mint
>>>>>    - Crispy duck breast dusted with lilac salt blend, with pear jam,
>>>>>    celeriac purÃ©e, confit garlic butter kale, and crispy potato
>>>>>
>>>>> *4.*
>>>>>
>>>>>    - CrÃ¨me brÃ»lÃ©e tart with blueberry and young mint
>>>>>    - Melon buckle cake with marinated melon, whipped cream, and mint
>>>>>    - Chocolate lava cake with strawberry and gelato
>>>>>    - Cornbread pudding with marinated blueberry and gelato
>>>>>
>>>>> *Cannabis Addition:*
>>>>>
>>>>> DF Private Chef cannot supply cannabis, but we are experts in
>>>>> preparing it for consumption.

>>>>>
>>>>>    - If you are visiting ATG in Salisbury, please visit ATG Salisbury
>>>>>    <https://dutchie.com/embedded-menu/alternative-therapies-group-salisbury/products/concentrates>
>>>>>    .

>>>>>    selection. Silver Therapeutics Berwick
>>>>>    <https://www.leafly.com/dispensary-info/silver-therapeutics---berwick/menu?product_category%5B%5D=Concentrate&product_category%5B%5D=Other>
>>>>>    .
>>>>>
>>>>> When your chef arrives, please make the concentrate available. In
>>>>> return, we will convert it into an oil-based tincture ready for consumption.
>>>>>
>>>>> We advise purchasing only concentrate, as it offers a direct
>>>>> conversion for making edibles. Flower wouldnât provide definite results in
>>>>> time.
>>>>>
>>>>> If the store you visit has tinctures, we strongly advise not buying
>>>>> them. We will make a much stronger tincture for the same price! Most
>>>>> store-bought tinctures don't reach high strength, maxing at 300mg. Any
>>>>> concentrate you buy will be 70-85% per gram, which can be converted into a
>>>>> 700-850mg tincture. This gives us full control of the product and leaves
>>>>> you with a whole tincture when we leave.
>>>>>
>>>>>

fixtures.body:
Amazing!
1. mixed green salad w/ blueberry vinaigrette, croutons + mozzarella
Hoping to maybe do both dishes for 2? idk if theres a way to do that, but I could see her loving both!
2. tuna crudo w/ cucumber + radish dressed purple pepper + basil vinaigrette
or
crab cakes w/ lemon butter sauce + zucchini
3. crispy duck breast served w/ crunchy potatoes, seasonal greens, preserved + raw stone fruit
4. chocolate lava cake with strawberry and gelato
Canât wait! Thank you so much for being willing to make her bday so special!
Warmly,
Dana Epstein

fixtures.body:
Yes, this is fine. It won't be too much, no worries at all! I can totally
make it happen. ð
We have you down for a 5 course menu (1 salad, 2 app, 1 entree, 1 dessert
-split)
I would need the final head count by Friday. I want to be sure to hit the
markets!
I normally charge 160/per person for a 4 course meal. Being how the menu is
set up, I won't have to adjust the price.
I'm sorry for making this feel rushed as the event is a few days away. I do
require 50% deposit upon booking, then the rest 2 days before the event
date.
If you have any questions or need assistance, don't hesitate to ask me.
I can accept Venmo or you can use your card on my site.
The amount sue would be 480 or 640.
https://venmo.com/code?user_id=3286910017470464648
https://www.dfprivatechef.com/book-now

fixtures.subject: Re: Private Dinner - August
fixtures.body:
Hi David!!!
Looking forward to tomorrow! I just wanted to give you a heads up that my
mom prefers her duck cooked through, more done medium/well done,  basically
just not any rareness. Otherwise so excited for this!!!!! Wahoo!
Address: 6 Robin Way, Ogunquit ME.
Her house is right next to the Admiralâs Inn (Yellow hotel). There is a
main driveway and then a parking area owned by us to the left of the house,
feel free to park there! My fiancÃ© Mark will be there to greet you and help
you! Any questions feel free to call or text 508-404-4542.
Warmly,
Dana Epstein

fixtures.subject: Private Chef Inquiry - July 9th Dinner - Tuftonboro, NH
fixtures.body:
Hi there!
Im reaching out to inquire about private chef services for a special event were hosting on July 9th at our company property in Tuftonboro, NH : https://www.emberescapes.com/
Our team will be gathering at Quietude from July 8th10th for a few days of leadership development and team bonding. Since we work remotely and typically only see each other while traveling for events, this retreat offers a rare and meaningful opportunity to connect in person.
On the evening of July 9th, we will be honoring the recipient of our annual Grace Award, which recognizes our teams MVP. Wed love to make this moment even more special with a beautifully prepared three-course dinner for 18 hospitality professionals and meeting planners.
Were looking for a chef who can:
  *   Use our fully equipped kitchen at the property
  *   Plan, cook, and serve a three-course celebratory meal
  *   Optionally prepare breakfast or light items for the following day, if interested (was thinking a few breakfast casseroles that we can pop in the oven)
If this sounds like a good fit, Id love to schedule a video call to discuss details further.
Looking forward to hearing from you!
Colleen

Hi there!
I’m reaching out to inquire about private chef services for a special event we’re hosting on July 9th at our company property in Tuftonboro, NH : https://www.emberescapes.com/
Our team will be gathering at Quietude from July 8th–10th for a few days of leadership development and team bonding. Since we work remotely and typically only see each other while traveling for events, this retreat offers a rare and meaningful opportunity to connect in person.
On the evening of July 9th, we will be honoring the recipient of our annual “Grace Award,” which recognizes our team’s MVP. We’d love to make this moment even more special with a beautifully prepared three-course dinner for 18 hospitality professionals and meeting planners.
We’re looking for a chef who can:
  *   Use our fully equipped kitchen at the property
  *   Plan, cook, and serve a three-course celebratory meal
  *   Optionally prepare breakfast or light items for the following day, if interested (was thinking a few breakfast casseroles that we can pop in the oven)

Colleen

fixtures.subject: Re: Private Chef Inquiry - July 9th Dinner - Tuftonboro, NH
fixtures.body:
Hi David,
Thanks for the call. Here are some more details about our dinner. If you don’t mind sending over a sample menu (doesn’t have to be fully custom for us yet), and your pricing, that’d be super helpful for me to go back to my bosses with. As I touched on, the chef we typically use at Quietude is moving away, so there’s some opportunity for you to become very popular at our property! We’re a group of event/meeting professionals, lots of us have backgrounds in catering/food service, just as an FYI 😊!
Wednesday, July 9th – Tuftonboro NH, at our property, Quietude<https://www.emberescapes.com/>.
Estimate timing – dinner at 7pm – you can literally come start prepping anytime that day…it’s your kitchen to use (and we will be in prof. development sessions all day)
18 guests – dietary restrictions (a lot more than I thought, sorry!)
  *   1 vegan (Sharmila)
  *   1 pescatarian (Colleen)
  *   1 GF, DF (Jess)
  *   2 no red meat (Kathy, Erin)
  *   1 shellfish allergy (Alex O)
  *   3 No Seafood (Krista, Will, Erin)
 Last year we did a family style menu – I believe it was this:
[cid:image010.png@01DBD3C8.4617DAD0]
July 10th – breakfast casseroles or breakfast sandwiches/burritos, etc. (something you can leave for us to heat up in the morning)
  *   We’ll be going on a couple hikes so we need a lot of fuel! Open to suggestions here…
  *   Maybe show this as optional with pricing so we can decide?
I hope this is enough info to get something started for us. We’re looking forward to seeing what you come up with.
Thanks again David!
Colleen

fixtures.subject: Re: Private Chef Inquiry - July 9th Dinner - Tuftonboro, NH
fixtures.body:
Hey Colleen,
Thank you so much for everything you provided! Im working on the menu, I'll
make sure to have it ready by the 10th.
I'm going to price the dinner at 100 a person, but normally I do charge
more. I can include my normal pricing just so you know for future
reference, if we decide to take on any endeavors.
As for breakfast I would say I do normally charge as well. Following what
we have discussed, Id be happy to leave a ready-to-eat meal for 20-30 a
head depending on the food.
I'll make some options for each course to narrow down with your team. I'll
also provide a whole separate vegan menu, if you decide you want to go that
way. I don't exactly type up all my menus, since I do have repeat clients
with less formal intentions. So they do vary from BOH to FOH looking.

fixtures.subject: Re: Private Chef Inquiry - July 9th Dinner - Tuftonboro, NH
fixtures.body:
Hey Colleen,
Thank you for your patience as I get into my busy season. I have all the
allergies noted.
I recommend narrowing dinner down to a three-course meal. If youâre excited
about adding any extra courses you see on the menu, just let me know!
As we discussed on the phone, I can do $100 per person for the dinner. This
is a lower rate for me, in hopes that we can turn this into something more
down the line. I'm currently managing a great relationship with a Airbnb
Host who has several properties in Maine, in which I have consistent
bookings with. I'd always be glad to connect you with him as a reference.
For the breakfast and the hiking snack, $25 a person for one option of
each. If youâd like to add more than that, just let me know and Iâll make
it work.
Thank you!
*summer 2025 menu*
*app*
   -
   melon + cucumber gazpacho, local feta
   -
   smoked peppers filled w/ goat cream cheese, black walnuts, lilac garlic
   vinaigrette
   -
   grilled stone fruit, arugula, fresh ricotta, preserved pepper vinaigrette
   -
   summer salad + blueberry balsamic vin + fresh mozzarella
   -
   cucumber + pea salad w/ brown butter lemon vinaigrette + manchego
*main*
   -
   bbq steak tips, buttered asparagus, braised tomatoes, grilled potato
   salad
   -
   blackberry smoke + braised short rib w/ grilled vegetable salad +
   cornbread
   -
   roasted chicken + herb jus w/ burnt onion, turnips + marinated cherry
   -
   chicken roulade stuffed w/ tomato, basil + asparagus + crispy potato
   -
   crispy duck + peach mostarda, sauteed broccolini, burnt onion + herb
   broth
   -
   haddock + herb crumb w/ sauteed zucchini, pea shoots + basil butter
   -
   salmon broiled w/ black pepper maple, sauteed beans + herb rice
   -
   scallops pan-seared w/ tomato + spinach, cauliflower parm puree
*dessert*
   -
   basque cheesecake, sweet fern crust + marinated berries
   -
   peach buckwheat buckle cake, lilac icing, cheesecake gelato
   -
   vanilla custard, marinated stone fruit + candied black walnut
   -
   strawberry shortcake + candied lime balm
   -
   chocolate + hazelnut bread pudding w/ chocolate ganache + raspberry
   -
   chocoflan + chocolate chip gelato
*breakfast*
   -
   breakfast burritos: bacon, scrambled eggs, cheese, caramelized onion +
   pepper
   -
      *vegan*: fried tofu, cashew pimento, caramelized onion + pepper
      -
   hashbrown casseroles: shredded potato, cheese, sausage, broccoli + egg
   -
      *vegan*: shredded potato, cheese, seitan chorizo, broccoli
      -
   fruit salad: chopped fruit, local honey + raspberry puree
   -
   choice of muffin:
   -
      blueberry muffin w/ lemon lilac butter
      -
      chocolate muffin w/ banana foster butter
      -
      peach muffin w/ cinnamon butter
      -
      coffee cake muffin w/ coffee butter
      -
      corn muffin w/ berry butter
      -
   yogurt parfait w/ marinated berries + nuts
*hiking snack*
   -
   nut butter + oat energy bar w/ dried berries + dark chocolate
   -
   ribeye jerky: maple bbq
   -
   fruit leather: mixed fruit
   -
   trail mix: mixed nuts, dried fruit + dark chocolate
------------------------------
*vegan summer 2025 menu*
*(all vegan cheese is homemade)*
*app*
   -
   melon + cucumber gazpacho, cashew feta
   -
   smoked peppers filled w/ almond cheese + black walnuts, lilac garlic
   vinaigrette
   -
   grilled stone fruit, arugula, soft tofu, preserved pepper vinaigrette
   -
   summer salad + blueberry balsamic vin + silky tofu
   -
   cucumber + pea salad w/ brown butter lemon vinaigrette + moroccan almond
*main*
   -
   bbq veggie skewers, buttered asparagus, grilled potato salad
   -
   blackberry smoke + braised jackfruit w/ grilled vegetable salad +
   cornbread
   -
   roasted beets + jus w/ burnt onion, turnips + marinated cherry
   -
   mushroom roulade stuffed w/ tomato, basil + asparagus + crispy potato
   -
   slow-braised rutabaga + peach mostarda, sauteed broccolini, burnt onion
   + herb broth
   -
   braised eggplant + herb crumb w/ sauteed zucchini, pea shoots + basil
   butter
   -
   turnips pan-seared w/ tomato + spinach, cauliflower puree
*dessert*
   -
   macadamia nut basque cheesecake, sweet fern crust + marinated berries
   -
   peach buckwheat buckle cake, lilac icing, peach sorbet
   -
   marinated stone fruit, whipped coconut cream + candied black walnut
   -
   strawberry shortcake + candied lime balm
   -
   chocolate + hazelnut bread pudding w/ chocolate ganache + raspberry
   -
   chocoflan + chocolate chip gelato

fixtures.body:
Hi David -
Thanks for sending over your menu. The team loved it and wanted me to ask a few clarifying questions.
  1.  Does your $100/pp include service charges and taxes/fees? Can we please see a full proposal with those items plus any other fees listed out?
  2.  I believe our menu would be the following:
     *   summer salad + blueberry balsamic vin + fresh mozzarella
     *   blackberry smoke + braised short rib w/ grilled vegetable salad + cornbread
     *   peach buckwheat buckle cake, lilac icing, cheesecake gelato
  *   We would want to know what the option would be for the dietary people – 2 guests don’t eat red meat – could they do a chicken
  *   What would you have the vegan/pescatarian eat?
  1.  I was reminded that last year we did a little happy hour at our on-site Shed/Bar before dinner. Would you be able to add a grazing table of some sort? (I know our price would go up)
  2.  Would you have anyone with you to help with service?
  3.  Where would you be coming from? NH? Maine?
Thank you!!
Colleen

fixtures.subject: Re: Private Chef Inquiry - July 9th Dinner - Tuftonboro, NH
fixtures.body:
Hi Colleen,
Dinner is $100 per person. Thereâs no added service or travel fee. Iâm
based in Waterford, Maine, which is just over an hour from you. I also have
places I stay all over New England depending on where my dinners are, so
the travel is no issue for me. This is a normal drive.
The only additional charge is 5.5 percent Maine sales tax.
For service, it will be me and one other chef. We donât hire outside
servers. All food and service is handled directly by us.
For the two guests who donât eat red meat, Iâll prepare chicken. The vegan
guest will get a plated jackfruit dish, and the pescatarian will have a
seafood option. If they have a preference for the type of fish, just let me
know and Iâll make sure they get something theyâll enjoy.
I can also put together a grazing table before dinner. I use local
ingredients, so itâs more involved than a basic cheese board. I can offer
that at $20 per person.
Hereâs the full price breakdown:
Dinner (July 9 for 18 guests)
3 course dinner at $100 per person
Includes food, service for two people, and travel
Maine sales tax at 5.5 percent applies
Dinner subtotal: $1800
Sales tax: $99
Total: $1899
Breakfast and Hiking Snack (July 10)
One breakfast and one snack per guest at $25
Total: $450
Optional Grazing Table (Pre Dinner)
$20 per person
Optional total: $360
Total with Grazing Table: $2709
Let me know if anything needs adjusting and Iâll send over the final
invoice.
-Chef Ferragamo

Hi David,
Did I not respond to this? So sorry…
We are printing menus – can you confirm what the chicken and fish dishes will be?
We are going to move forward with the cheese grazing board, but remove the breakfast/hiking snack, please.
Can we pay you via ACH? We don’t do Venmo I found out.
Thanks!

From: David Ferragamo <dfprivatechef@gmail.com>
Date: Wednesday, June 11, 2025 at 5:31 PM
To: Colleen Hartigan <colleen@emberbrandfire.com>
Subject: Re: Private Chef Inquiry - July 9th Dinner - Tuftonboro, NH
Hi Colleen,
Dinner is $100 per person. There’s no added service or travel fee. I’m based in Waterford, Maine, which is just over an hour from you. I also have places I stay all over New England depending on where my dinners are, so the travel is no issue for me. This is a normal drive.
The only additional charge is 5.5 percent Maine sales tax.
For service, it will be me and one other chef. We don’t hire outside servers. All food and service is handled directly by us.
For the two guests who don’t eat red meat, I’ll prepare chicken. The vegan guest will get a plated jackfruit dish, and the pescatarian will have a seafood option. If they have a preference for the type of fish, just let me know and I’ll make sure they get something they’ll enjoy.
I can also put together a grazing table before dinner. I use local ingredients, so it’s more involved than a basic cheese board. I can offer that at $20 per person.
Here’s the full price breakdown:
Dinner (July 9 for 18 guests)
3 course dinner at $100 per person
Includes food, service for two people, and travel
Maine sales tax at 5.5 percent applies
Dinner subtotal: $1800
Sales tax: $99
Total: $1899
Breakfast and Hiking Snack (July 10)
One breakfast and one snack per guest at $25
Total: $450
Optional Grazing Table (Pre Dinner)
$20 per person
Optional total: $360
Total with Grazing Table: $2709
Let me know if anything needs adjusting and I’ll send over the final invoice.
-Chef Ferragamo

fixtures.subject: Re: Private Chef Inquiry - July 9th Dinner - Tuftonboro, NH
fixtures.body:
Hey there –
Just wanted to confirm we are all good for Wednesday. Here are some last-minute details. I’m heading up to the property later today but feel free to call me at any time with questions! 508-277-8784.
  *   Arrival time – any time after 1pm is fine with us. We will be on property, but you’ll have the kitchen to yourself.
  *   Cocktail hour (grazing board) will be in the Library (2 rooms over from the kitchen) at 5:00pm. We’ve assigned Erin on our team to help you with anything you need.
  *   Dinner will be in the Dining Room (attached to the kitchen) at 7:00pm. We have all the chinaware for you and we’ll set the table.
  *   Would you be able to announce/talk about each course? We love an interactive chef! Plus, as I mentioned previously, we’re looking for a new chef to be on our pamphlet to offer as an option for our guests to hire. Our two owners Chris and Kathy, and then our CEO Grace (who’s a major foodie) will all be sitting for dinner, and I’m sure will ask questions 😊
  *   I’m sure you will be, but please be prepared to be in front of guests (tidy and professional) and keep the kitchen as tidy as you can while you’re working. It’s possible that we’ll be in and out of there to get things, etc.

fixtures.subject: Private Dinner | October 17
fixtures.body:
Hey Jenna,
I apperciate your patience as we get your dinner together. Please
confirm/fill out the information below.
Price: $350
Date: Oct 17
Location: 19 Prentiss St, Old Town, ME
Guest Count: 2
Serving Style: 4-Course Plated
Serving Time: (Chef will arrive 2hrs early.)
Occasion: 2 Year Anniversary Dinner
Allergies or Restrictions:
All payments will be made upon confirming your booking. I sent over
previous selections to create a 4-course menu. I revised it to create
courses that lend well with lobster being the main. I hope you enjoy them.
I can always create anything else or give you more options.
1. Squash, Spinach +Almond Terrine w/ Monchego Lemon Sauce
2. Braised Tomato w/ Carmelized Celeriac Soup + Thyme
3. Whole Lobster w/ Breadcrumbed Tail, Butter + Steamed Broccoli (the rest
will be inside shell and cracked)
4. Chocolate Mousse Pomegranate Pie w/ White Chocolate Sorbet
- Chef Ferragamo

fixtures.subject: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
fixtures.body:
Hi David, my name is Jodi Hoffmann and I'm one of the gals in Lynne's group
renting the Quietude home for the weekend of October 4th.  Since I'm the
"foodie" of the group, Lynne asked me if I'd take on the details for our
dinner that we'd love for you to prepare for us.
Is it possible for you to send me some sample menus that correspond with
your course pricing?  I'd love for the ladies to weigh in with their
thoughts and preferences and the menus would be a huge help.
The only allergy that I'm aware of at the moment is one gal has a
shellfish allergy, but I'll follow up on that.
Thank you!
Jodi
203 218-2312

Hey Jodi, It's nice to meet you! I can certainly give you some sample
menus.
Thank you for the feedback on allergies. Everything will be taken into
consideration with what you decide on. ð
Althought everything is custom, these are many of the dishes I've done this
summer. I'm always taking suggestions, dislikes or favorites in mind while
making these menus. We will be entering spooky season too, I always have
cool things I can make.
- Chef Ferragamo
*Summer Menu*
VEG / APP
â¢ SautÃ©ed snap pea & broccolini salad with brown butter vinaigrette,
manchego
â¢ Grilled stone fruit, arugula, vegan ricotta, preserved pepper vinaigrette
â¢ Summer salad with blueberry balsamic vinaigrette and fresh mozzarella
â¢ Chopped beet, spinach & charred onion salad with black pepper blueberry
vinaigrette, dried lime balm, goat cheese
â¢ Tomato gazpacho with crispy shallot oil, basil, pruned blueberry capers
â¢ Smoked peppers filled with goat cream cheese, black walnuts, lilac garlic
vinaigrette
â¢ Strawberry carpaccio with charred corn relish, goat cheese, herbs
â¢ Tomato, arugula & herb salad with charred strawberry balsamic
vinaigrette, mint, tarragon
â¢ Melon & cucumber gazpacho with local feta
â¢ Cucumber & pea salad with brown butter lemon vinaigrette, manchego
â¢ Grilled Texas toast with whipped herb goat cheese, pepper jelly, raw
zucchini salad
â¢ Grilled & charred eggplant with pickled giardiniera, basil croutons, feta
â¢ Fried pickles with fermented garlic aioli, chopped chives
â¢ Bloominâ onion with horseradish aioli, sour cream chive seasoning
MAIN
â¢ Marinated grilled NY sirloin with smoked tomato butter, creamed spinach,
crispy garlic
â¢ BBQ steak tips with buttered asparagus, braised tomatoes, grilled potato
salad
â¢ Blackberry smoked & braised short rib with grilled vegetable salad,
cornbread
â¢ Chicken roulade stuffed with tomato, basil, asparagus, crispy potato
â¢ Roasted chicken with herb jus, burnt onion, turnips, marinated cherry
â¢ Crispy duck with peach mostarda, sautÃ©ed broccolini, burnt onion, herb
broth
â¢ Haddock with herb crumb, sautÃ©ed zucchini, pea shoots, basil butter
â¢ Salmon with black pepper maple glaze, sautÃ©ed beans, herb rice
â¢ Scallops pan-seared with tomato & spinach, cauliflower parm purÃ©e
â¢ Butter-poached lobster roll with lemon chive aioli, hand-cut tossed
fries, and house slaw
DESSERT
â¢ Basque cheesecake with sweet fern crust, marinated berries
â¢ Peach custard tart with white tea gelato
â¢ Peach buckwheat buckle cake with lilac icing, cheesecake gelato
â¢ Vanilla custard with marinated stone fruit, candied black walnut
â¢ Strawberry shortcake with candied lime balm
â¢ Chocolate & hazelnut bread pudding with chocolate ganache, raspberry
â¢ Chocoflan with chocolate chip gelato
â¢ Chocolate lava cake with seasonal fruit, gelato
I've even been doing lots of brick oven pizzas, I could definitely cook a
few for yall of that was done you had in mind. I have my own portable pizza
oven.
*Pricing:*
Couples (Two Guests)
3 Courses â $200/person ($400 total)
4 Courses â $250/person ($500 total)
5 Courses â $300/person ($600 total)
Large Tasting Menu (8â15+ courses) â Custom rates
Groups of 3+
3 Courses â $155/person
4 Courses â $185/person
5 Courses â $215/person
Large Tasting Menu (8â15+ courses) Custom rates
All
 Brick-Fired Pizza Deal â $150/person
 (Pizza + salad served together, dessert left for guests â more hands-off
service)

fixtures.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
fixtures.body:
Good Morning David,
I've added Lynne to our email chain, now that she is not traveling.  I'm
circling back with you to see if you've had a chance to come up with a menu
proposal that is in keeping with our $115 per person budget since we last
talked on Aug. 25th.
Thank you!
Jodi

fixtures.body:
Hey! I really appreciate your patience. I hope that you like these
selections!
Let me know which 3 courses your group would like to move forward with.
*Appetizers & Starters*
   1.
   Roasted squash & spinach terrine w/ manchego cream + almonds
   2.
   Butternut squash soup w/ spiced chamomile cream, crispy shallots + onion
   toast
   3.
   Corn bisque w/ burnt onion + thyme buttermilk
   4.
   Cream of cauliflower soup w/ burnt onion + thyme
   5.
   Fall salad w/ wilted spinach, walnuts, caramelized onion vinaigrette,
   dehydrated cranberries or pears, baked brie
   6.
   Fried cauliflower & potato ball w/ herb aioli, local curds, crispy leeks
   7.
   Grilled pears w/ hyssop ricotta, summer pickled vinaigrette + lettuce mix
   8.
   Grilled peppers stuffed w/ ricottaâwalnut blend, burnt maple butter sauce
   9.
   Blooming onion w/ fresh ribeye jerky + horseradish herb cream
   10.
   Mushroom ravioli w/ brown sage butter, creamed mushroom + spinach,
   chestnut crumble
------------------------------
*EntrÃ©es (Main Courses)*
   1.
   Filet mignon w/ confit garlic butter + parsnip purÃ©e, seasonal green
   2.
   Breaded Rosemary Chicken w/ Savory Roasted Apple w/ cauliflower purÃ©e,
   brown butter, seasonal green + local sumac
   3.
   Braised Chicken + Hickory Nut Romesco w/ caramelized celeriac root +
   cabbage, radish
   4.
   Braised lamb w/ brown butter lemon sauce, capers, zucchini + eggplant
   5.
   Braised short rib w/ pumpkin barbecue glaze, grilled vegetable salad,
   slow-cooked sweet potato, yogurt
   6.
   Crispy salmon over farro risotto, roasted pumpkin, sage + wilted greens
   7.
   Pan-seared scallops w/ garden summer garden relish, corn purÃ©e, burnt
   onions + fried potatoes
   8.
   Scallops pan-seared w/ tomato + spinach, cauliflower parm purÃ©e
   9.
   Lobster tail w/ lemon beurre blanc, cauliflower purÃ©e, seasonal green
   10.
   Butter-poached lobster + lobster bisque w/ asparagus + zucchini roulade,
   crunchy potato
------------------------------
*Desserts*
   1.
   Chocolate lava cake w/ seasonal fruit + gelato
   2.
   Marble cheesecake brownie w/ chocolate mousse + roasted berries
   3.
   Basque cheesecake w/ sweet fern crust + peaches
   4.
   Upside-down pear cake w/ salted caramel + vanilla sumac gelato
   5.
   Pumpkin cinnamon roll w/ cinnamon gelato + candied pears
   6.
   Carrot cake w/ root beer caramel + carrot cake ice cream
   7.
   Vanilla & grape custard w/ chai candied pumpkin granola
   8.
   Baked apples w/ whipped cinnamon ricotta + toasted walnuts
   9.
   Chocolate & hazelnut bread pudding w/ preserved raspberries + candied
   hickory nuts
   10.
   Halloween dish (passion fruit sorbet, black licorice + chocolate cookie)

dates.context: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4 Hey! I really appreciate your patience.

fixtures.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
fixtures.body:
Hi David,
Thanks for sending the new menu options.  I believed I mentioned that our
group would prefer to forgo dessert and have one appetizer and a salad, so
would you please confirm that you'd be ok with this?  Also, given that one
of our gals has a shellfish allergy, we've decided it would be best not to
offer up any of the seafood suggestions, although they all
sound delicious!  Would you consider offering us a choice of two different
entree options, so that those who would like beef could have it and those
who would like chicken could have their choice?  I suspect this would
complicate things a bit and would understand if you would prefer to just
prepare one entree for our group.  Lastly, is the breaded rosemary chicken
a boneless, skinless chicken breast?
I will be away from Sept 15th - 27th, so please hit "reply all" so Lynne
can pick up my slack.  I would like to have this all figured out before I
leave and send you a deposit if you require it, so please let me know about
that as well.
Thank you!
Jodi

fixtures.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
fixtures.body:
Hey Jodi,
Thank you for all the feedback, I'd be totally alright with the chicken and
beef as long as we keep the sides the same. It would be harder to juggle to
separate meals. I can make the chicken skinless if that is what you are
looking for. That's no problem at all!

dates.context: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4 Hey Jodi, Thank you for all the feedbac

fixtures.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
fixtures.body:
Hi David,
Thank you for being so amenable to offering us a choice of entrees.  It is
very much appreciated!   With regards to a chicken option, we would like a
boneless, skinless chicken breast, so wondering if you could do a lemon
chicken or maybe even a picatta?  I currently have only a partial tally of
entree preference; just waiting on two more women to weigh in.  Once I have
their choices, I will let you know.
Also, as I'm currently out of the country, if you'd like a deposit in
advance of our dinner, please reach out to Lynne Pinto as she has
graciously offered to step in for me while I'm away.  We're very much
looking forward to our evening with you!
Jodi

fixtures.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
fixtures.body:
Yes that sounds great to me, whatever you're thinking, I can make. Not sure
if picatta would be great with steak so I'll provide a different sauce with
the beef. ð
Thank you! Don't worry about the deposit, we are still figuring out the
meal. I really appreciate it.

Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4 Hey David, the piccata sauce is for the

fixtures.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
fixtures.body:
Hey David,
Not sure if you saw my email above, but now that we're less than a week
out, I thought I'd circle back with you as I feel there might be a
disconnect here.  We did not expect you to give each person both chicken
and beef.  My intent was to offer the girls either the chicken or the
beef.  Our count is 6 beef entrees and 3 chicken entrees.
We would like to begin our happy hour at 5:30 so given that, what time
would you like to arrive?  Do you need us to do anything in advance of your
arrival?  A couple of us are supplementing the happy hour nibbles, so I
will already have the oven preheated to 350.
Just confirming that the price per person is $115.
If you would like to be paid in cash, we will need advanced warning.
Otherwise, we can pay you via venmo or zelle.  Does that work for you?
We're looking forward to our evening!
Thanks,
Jodi

fixtures.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
fixtures.body:
Hey Jodi,
Thanks for following up, and I appreciate your patience.
Iâve got the headcount at 9 people total (6 beef entrÃ©es and 3 chicken
entrÃ©es). Letâs set it at a flat rate of $1,000 for the evening. You can
send that to me via Venmo: venmo.com/u/DFPrivateChef.
Iâll plan to arrive around 4:30â5:00 to get set up before happy hour at
5:30. Do you prefer dinner service to start closer to 6:00 or 6:30? Either
works fine, and we can always finalize on the day of.
I donât need anything else on your end, and I really appreciate you having
the oven preheated to 350.
Looking forward to the evening!
- David

fixtures.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
fixtures.body:
Hi Jodi and Lynne,
As I was going back over my notes, I realized we didnât finalize the
appetizer and salad for your dinner. Everything else is confirmed: filet
with garlic confit butter or chicken piccata for the entrÃ©es, with
cauliflower purÃ©e, green beans, and charred onion as the sides.
When you get a chance, could you please pick one appetizer and one salad
from this list:
1. Roasted squash & spinach terrine with manchego cream and almonds
2. Butternut squash soup with spiced chamomile cream, crispy shallots and
onion toast
3. Corn bisque with burnt onion and thyme buttermilk
4. Cream of cauliflower soup with burnt onion and thyme
5. Fall salad with wilted spinach, walnuts, caramelized onion vinaigrette,
dehydrated cranberries or pears, baked brie
6. Fried cauliflower and potato ball with herb aioli, local curds, crispy
leeks
7. Grilled pears with hyssop ricotta, summer pickled vinaigrette and
lettuce mix
8. Grilled peppers stuffed with ricottaâwalnut blend, burnt maple butter
sauce
9. Blooming onion with fresh ribeye jerky and horseradish herb cream
10. Mushroom ravioli with brown sage butter, creamed mushroom and spinach,
chestnut crumble
My recommendation would be #1 for the appetizer, since it pairs really well
with your entrÃ©es, and #5 for the salad, which ties everything together
nicely. But of course, feel free to choose whichever two sound best to you.
Once you confirm, the full menu will be locked in. Looking forward to it.
David

Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4 Hi Jodi and Lynne, As I was going back

fixtures.subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
fixtures.body:
Hi David,
I thought we discussed our appetizer and salad option on the phone quite a
while back (But maybe we didn't) and it remains the same:  for the
appetizer we liked the fried cauliflower and potato ball with the herb
aioli, local curds and crispy leeks.  If possible, we'd like to incorporate
that with our happy hour nibbles, so if possible, could they be ready at
5;30?  For salad, we'd like the fall salad with the wilted spinach.  I
returned home with a whopping head cold, so I will not be joining the girls
this weekend.  I had wanted the chicken piccata, so you only need to make 2
orders of that now.  I think we're good to go now!
Jodi

fixtures.subject: RE: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
fixtures.body:
Hi David, since Jodi won’t be with us at Quietude, I wanted to provide you with my cell phone # in case you need to be in touch:   513 307 8716.
If we are not at the house when you arrive, the code to get in is:  8716.
Thanks much and see you on Sat!
Lynne
From: Jodi Hoffmann <jodihoffmann11@gmail.com>
Sent: Thursday, October 2, 2025 8:35 PM
To: David Ferragamo <dfprivatechef@gmail.com>; Powellpinto, Lynne <powellpinto.le@pg.com>
Subject: Re: Lynne Pinto's dinner on 10/4
[EXTERNAL]
Hi David,
I thought we discussed our appetizer and salad option on the phone quite a while back (But maybe we didn't) and it remains the same:  for the appetizer we liked the fried cauliflower and potato ball with the herb aioli, local curds and crispy leeks.  If possible, we'd like to incorporate that with our happy hour nibbles, so if possible, could they be ready at 5;30?  For salad, we'd like the fall salad with the wilted spinach.  I returned home with a whopping head cold, so I will not be joining the girls this weekend.  I had wanted the chicken piccata, so you only need to make 2 orders of that now.  I think we're good to go now!
Jodi
On Thu, Oct 2, 2025 at 1:51 PM David Ferragamo <dfprivatechef@gmail.com<mailto:dfprivatechef@gmail.com>> wrote:
Hi Jodi and Lynne,
As I was going back over my notes, I realized we didn’t finalize the appetizer and salad for your dinner. Everything else is confirmed: filet with garlic confit butter or chicken piccata for the entrées, with cauliflower purée, green beans, and charred onion as the sides.
When you get a chance, could you please pick one appetizer and one salad from this list:
1. Roasted squash & spinach terrine with manchego cream and almonds
2. Butternut squash soup with spiced chamomile cream, crispy shallots and onion toast
3. Corn bisque with burnt onion and thyme buttermilk
4. Cream of cauliflower soup with burnt onion and thyme
5. Fall salad with wilted spinach, walnuts, caramelized onion vinaigrette, dehydrated cranberries or pears, baked brie
6. Fried cauliflower and potato ball with herb aioli, local curds, crispy leeks
7. Grilled pears with hyssop ricotta, summer pickled vinaigrette and lettuce mix
8. Grilled peppers stuffed with ricotta–walnut blend, burnt maple butter sauce
9. Blooming onion with fresh ribeye jerky and horseradish herb cream
10. Mushroom ravioli with brown sage butter, creamed mushroom and spinach, chestnut crumble
My recommendation would be #1 for the appetizer, since it pairs really well with your entrées, and #5 for the salad, which ties everything together nicely. But of course, feel free to choose whichever two sound best to you.
Once you confirm, the full menu will be locked in. Looking forward to it.
David
On Tue, Sep 30, 2025, 11:57 AM David Ferragamo <dfprivatechef@gmail.com<mailto:dfprivatechef@gmail.com>> wrote:
Hey Jodi,
Thanks for following up, and I appreciate your patience.
I’ve got the headcount at 9 people total (6 beef entrées and 3 chicken entrées). Let’s set it at a flat rate of $1,000 for the evening. You can send that to me via Venmo: venmo.com/u/DFPrivateChef<https://urldefense.com/v3/__http:/venmo.com/u/DFPrivateChef__;!!NgSi4SaN6ydtRVQ-!0wQ40ehQ12rL-7DkfMcEPtgtTFXZT4nxFXDX9K0bZ7ILdBLGxsI-fnDustXtv4gAR4k-JI8br-p3CAurHk9a3LLUYg$>.
I’ll plan to arrive around 4:30–5:00 to get set up before happy hour at 5:30. Do you prefer dinner service to start closer to 6:00 or 6:30? Either works fine, and we can always finalize on the day of.
I don’t need anything else on your end, and I really appreciate you having the oven preheated to 350.
Looking forward to the evening!
- David
On Mon, Sep 29, 2025, 4:35 PM Jodi Hoffmann <jodihoffmann11@gmail.com<mailto:jodihoffmann11@gmail.com>> wrote:
Hey David,
Not sure if you saw my email above, but now that we're less than a week out, I thought I'd circle back with you as I feel there might be a disconnect here.  We did not expect you to give each person both chicken and beef.  My intent was to offer the girls either the chicken or the beef.  Our count is 6 beef entrees and 3 chicken entrees.
We would like to begin our happy hour at 5:30 so given that, what time would you like to arrive?  Do you need us to do anything in advance of your arrival?  A couple of us are supplementing the happy hour nibbles, so I will already have the oven preheated to 350.
Just confirming that the price per person is $115.
If you would like to be paid in cash, we will need advanced warning.  Otherwise, we can pay you via venmo or zelle.  Does that work for you?
We're looking forward to our evening!
Thanks,
Jodi
On Sat, Sep 20, 2025 at 4:26 PM Jodi Hoffmann <jodihoffmann11@gmail.com<mailto:jodihoffmann11@gmail.com>> wrote:
Hey David, the piccata sauce is for the chicken (only) and for the filet we like your choice of the garlic confit butter.  Are you still ok with making the two different entrees?
Jodi

Yes that sounds great to me, whatever you're thinking, I can make. Not sure if picatta would be great with steak so I'll provide a different sauce with the beef. 😊
Thank you! Don't worry about the deposit, we are still figuring out the meal. I really appreciate it.
On Thu, Sep 18, 2025, 5:17 AM Jodi Hoffmann <jodihoffmann11@gmail.com<mailto:jodihoffmann11@gmail.com>> wrote:
Hi David,
Thank you for being so amenable to offering us a choice of entrees.  It is very much appreciated!   With regards to a chicken option, we would like a boneless, skinless chicken breast, so wondering if you could do a lemon chicken or maybe even a picatta?  I currently have only a partial tally of entree preference; just waiting on two more women to weigh in.  Once I have their choices, I will let you know.
Also, as I'm currently out of the country, if you'd like a deposit in advance of our dinner, please reach out to Lynne Pinto as she has graciously offered to step in for me while I'm away.  We're very much looking forward to our evening with you!
Jodi
On Wed, Sep 10, 2025 at 10:02 PM David Ferragamo <dfprivatechef@gmail.com<mailto:dfprivatechef@gmail.com>> wrote:
Hey Jodi,
Thank you for all the feedback, I'd be totally alright with the chicken and beef as long as we keep the sides the same. It would be harder to juggle to separate meals. I can make the chicken skinless if that is what you are looking for. That's no problem at all!
On Sun, Sep 7, 2025, 5:58 PM Jodi Hoffmann <jodihoffmann11@gmail.com<mailto:jodihoffmann11@gmail.com>> wrote:
Hi David,
Thanks for sending the new menu options.  I believed I mentioned that our group would prefer to forgo dessert and have one appetizer and a salad, so would you please confirm that you'd be ok with this?  Also, given that one of our gals has a shellfish allergy, we've decided it would be best not to offer up any of the seafood suggestions, although they all sound delicious!  Would you consider offering us a choice of two different entree options, so that those who would like beef could have it and those who would like chicken could have their choice?  I suspect this would complicate things a bit and would understand if you would prefer to just prepare one entree for our group.  Lastly, is the breaded rosemary chicken a boneless, skinless chicken breast?
I will be away from Sept 15th - 27th, so please hit "reply all" so Lynne can pick up my slack.  I would like to have this all figured out before I leave and send you a deposit if you require it, so please let me know about that as well.
Thank you!
Jodi

On Tue, Aug 19, 2025 at 11:51 PM David Ferragamo <dfprivatechef@gmail.com<mailto:dfprivatechef@gmail.com>> wrote:
Hey Jodi, It's nice to meet you! I can certainly give you some sample menus.
Thank you for the feedback on allergies. Everything will be taken into consideration with what you decide on. 😊
Althought everything is custom, these are many of the dishes I've done this summer. I'm always taking suggestions, dislikes or favorites in mind while making these menus. We will be entering spooky season too, I always have cool things I can make.
- Chef Ferragamo
Summer Menu
VEG / APP
• Sautéed snap pea & broccolini salad with brown butter vinaigrette, manchego
• Grilled stone fruit, arugula, vegan ricotta, preserved pepper vinaigrette
• Summer salad with blueberry balsamic vinaigrette and fresh mozzarella
• Chopped beet, spinach & charred onion salad with black pepper blueberry vinaigrette, dried lime balm, goat cheese
• Tomato gazpacho with crispy shallot oil, basil, pruned blueberry capers
• Smoked peppers filled with goat cream cheese, black walnuts, lilac garlic vinaigrette
• Strawberry carpaccio with charred corn relish, goat cheese, herbs
• Tomato, arugula & herb salad with charred strawberry balsamic vinaigrette, mint, tarragon
• Melon & cucumber gazpacho with local feta
• Cucumber & pea salad with brown butter lemon vinaigrette, manchego
• Grilled Texas toast with whipped herb goat cheese, pepper jelly, raw zucchini salad
• Grilled & charred eggplant with pickled giardiniera, basil croutons, feta
• Fried pickles with fermented garlic aioli, chopped chives
• Bloomin’ onion with horseradish aioli, sour cream chive seasoning
MAIN
• Marinated grilled NY sirloin with smoked tomato butter, creamed spinach, crispy garlic
• BBQ steak tips with buttered asparagus, braised tomatoes, grilled potato salad
• Blackberry smoked & braised short rib with grilled vegetable salad, cornbread
• Chicken roulade stuffed with tomato, basil, asparagus, crispy potato
• Roasted chicken with herb jus, burnt onion, turnips, marinated cherry
• Crispy duck with peach mostarda, sautéed broccolini, burnt onion, herb broth
• Haddock with herb crumb, sautéed zucchini, pea shoots, basil butter
• Salmon with black pepper maple glaze, sautéed beans, herb rice
• Scallops pan-seared with tomato & spinach, cauliflower parm purée
• Butter-poached lobster roll with lemon chive aioli, hand-cut tossed fries, and house slaw
DESSERT
• Basque cheesecake with sweet fern crust, marinated berries
• Peach custard tart with white tea gelato
• Peach buckwheat buckle cake with lilac icing, cheesecake gelato
• Vanilla custard with marinated stone fruit, candied black walnut
• Strawberry shortcake with candied lime balm
• Chocolate & hazelnut bread pudding with chocolate ganache, raspberry
• Chocoflan with chocolate chip gelato
• Chocolate lava cake with seasonal fruit, gelato
I've even been doing lots of brick oven pizzas, I could definitely cook a few for yall of that was done you had in mind. I have my own portable pizza oven.
Pricing:
Couples (Two Guests)
3 Courses — $200/person ($400 total)
4 Courses — $250/person ($500 total)
5 Courses — $300/person ($600 total)
Large Tasting Menu (8–15+ courses) — Custom rates
Groups of 3+
3 Courses — $155/person
4 Courses — $185/person
5 Courses — $215/person
Large Tasting Menu (8–15+ courses) Custom rates
All
 Brick-Fired Pizza Deal — $150/person
 (Pizza + salad served together, dessert left for guests — more hands-off service)

Hi David, my name is Jodi Hoffmann and I'm one of the gals in Lynne's group renting the Quietude home for the weekend of October 4th.  Since I'm the "foodie" of the group, Lynne asked me if I'd take on the details for our dinner that we'd love for you to prepare for us.
Is it possible for you to send me some sample menus that correspond with your course pricing?  I'd love for the ladies to weigh in with their thoughts and preferences and the menus would be a huge help.
The only allergy that I'm aware of at the moment is one gal has a shellfish allergy, but I'll follow up on that.
Thank you!
Jodi

fixtures.subject: Dinner Parties in Rockport/Camden: June 9 and June 16
fixtures.body:
Hey Hal,
Thank you for the picture of the kitchen. It helps us immesley for our
preparation.
Our cannabis dining is different from what we are allowed on Take-a-chef.
Thank you for showing interest.
Below is information regarding our dinners. Normally the Q+A is included
but I have your feedback already. Please give me 2-3 days and I'll have a
menu with options to refine available.
You requested a 4-course meal, then it started to sound like you wanted
family style once you learned about the cannabis addition.
When we hold our cannabis dinners, absolutely nothing is dosed beforehand.
This allows all guests to be able to choose their threshold or even opt out
completely.
We have a very intricate way of plating and guest interaction that allows
us to track + dose specific plates.
It is very important to us that all guests are allowed the same exact
experience, while also ensuring everyone's safety.
*Rates per person*
Normal | $200/4course, $240/8course, $260/12course
Cannabis | $250/4course, $275/8course, $300/12course
Guest count: min 5, max 20
*The party size must be confirmed up to 5 days before the event takes place.
*$20 up-charge per person for groups smaller than 5
*Day of Event*
We are a small team consisting of 1-3 chefs. Our team must have time to
arrive 3-4hrs early prior to dining. Upon arrival our crew will spend a
short time unloading equipment into your kitchen. We ask that all
countertop appliances must be removed to give us a full range of counter
space. It is appreciated that you provide a clean space for us to cook, in
which we will return the same. Thank you for your cooperation.
-Chef Ferragamo

fixtures.subject: Re: Dinner Parties in Rockport/Camden: June 9 and June 16
fixtures.body:
No worries! We can always arrange something in the future.
For the 16, you can feel free to book directly with me. Follow this link DF
Private Che <https://www.facebook.com/ChefFerragamo>f and click "Book
Appointment."
I have created two separate menus for you. Let me know if you'd like to
make any changes. Getting your specific requirements is important, thank
you for the patience.
Please note that slight changes could be made due to seasonal opportunity.
*June 9*
cucumber + bell pepper salad / smoked cherry goat cheese / white pine +
black pepper vin / pecan
basil salmon / zucchini + tomato gazpacho
bison or beef / pickled lilac peppercorns / asparagus / eggplant / sweet
onion
rhubarb, strawberry + goat cheesecake / walnut crust
*June 16*
radish, strawberry, tomato, fennel salad / manchego / crispy garlic scapes
poached crab / asparagus + pea / wheat tart / poppy seed dressing
beef / sweet potato / sauteed greens / peppers
sweet corn gelato / beet cocoa crumb / blueberry
It was fun to create these summer menus, we've been in such a winter
braising mode! Looking forward to hearing from you.
-Chef Ferragamo

fixtures.subject: Re: Dinner Parties in Rockport/Camden: June 9 and June 16
fixtures.body:
Hey Hal,
I'll gladly serve the soup first, no problem at all!
I have received your submission, thank you. All dinners will be paid 2
weeks prior to the event. I will be contacting you through Facebook
business for payment on May 26 for the first dinner. The following covers
all returns.
   - If guest cancels up to 14 days before the experience: guest will be
   entirely reinboursed
   - If guest cancels between 14 - 7 days prior to the experience: chef is
   entitled to 50% of the reservationsâ value.
   - If guest cancels with less than 7 days in advance: chef is entitled to
   the entire coverage of the experience
   - If chef cancels: guest will be entirely reimbursement
I have 2 local pork farmers waiting on my word! I have created some
pork/chicken courses for you. Please pick the dish you'd like to
move forward with and which menu you'd prefer it to be on.
 1. bbq pork or chicken burnt ends / corn + carmen peppers / summer pickles
2. brined + grilled pork chop or chicken thigh/ husk cherry jam / charred
onion / potato + seasonal green
3. stuffed pork loin or chicken breast / zucchini + bronz fennel / plum
Enjoy your Sunday! Chat soon.
- Chef Ferragamo

fixtures.body:
Chef Ferragamo,
Â Greetings.Â  I hope you are enjoying/enjoyed your weekend, depending upon when you read this.Â Â Â Thank you for providing pork and chicken courses for review.Â  They all look great, but after careful consideration, we would like the stuffed pork loin substituted for the bison/beef on June 9th.Â  As an aside, we love the fact that the pork will be sourced from a local pork farmer, as we like supporting the local economy.Â In that vein, I'm arranging for a singer/songwriter from Wiscasset, Kat Logan, to play instrumental music during dinner and to sing afterward.Â Lastly, I'm not sure we have established a time for dinner.Â  I'm thinking around 6:30 if that works for you all.Â I hope your brackets are fairing better than mine.Â Best,HalÂ Â Â

I have 2 local pork farmers waiting on my word! I have created some pork/chicken courses for you. Please pick the dish you'd like to moveÂ forward with and which menuÂ you'd prefer it to be on.Â Â Â 1. bbqÂ pork or chicken burnt ends / corn + carmen peppers / summer pickles
2. brined + grilled pork chop or chicken thigh/ husk cherry jam / charred onion / potato + seasonal green
3. stuffed pork loin or chicken breast / zucchini + bronz fennel / plum
Â
Enjoy your Sunday! Chat soon.
Â
- Chef FerragamoÂ
