No worries! We can always arrange something in the future.For the 16, you can feel free to book directly with me. Follow this link DF Private Chef and click "Book Appointment."Â I have created two separate menus for you. Let me know if you'd like to make any changes. Getting your specific requirements is important, thank you for the patience.Please note that slight changes could be made due to seasonal opportunity.Â June 9
cucumber + bell pepper salad / smoked cherry goat cheese / white pine + black pepper vin / pecan
Â
basil salmon / zucchini + tomato gazpachoÂ
bison or beef / pickled lilac peppercorns / asparagus / eggplant / sweet onionÂ

June 16

In a message dated 3/12/2022 7:00:16 AM Central Standard Time, dfprivatechef@gmail.com writes:Â
Hey Hal,Â Thank you for the picture of the kitchen. It helps us immesley for our preparation.Our cannabis dining is different from what we are allowed on Take-a-chef. Thank you for showing interest.Below is information regarding our dinners. Normally the Q+A is included but I have your feedback already. Please give me 2-3 days and I'll have a menu with options to refine available.Â You requested a 4-course meal, then it started to sound like you wanted family style once you learned about the cannabis addition.When we hold our cannabis dinners, absolutely nothing is dosed beforehand. This allows all guests to be able to choose their threshold or even opt out completely.We have a very intricate way of plating and guest interaction that allows us to track + dose specific plates.It is very important to us that all guests are allowed the same exact experience, while also ensuring everyone's safety.Â Rates per person
Normal | $200/4course, $240/8course, $260/12courseCannabis | $250/4course, $275/8course, $300/12courseÂ
Guest count: min 5, max 20
*The party size must be confirmed up to 5 days before the event takes place.
*$20 up-charge per person for groups smaller than 5
Â
Day of Event
We are a small team consisting of 1-3 chefs. Our team must have time to arrive 3-4hrs early prior to dining. Upon arrival our crew will spend a short time unloading equipment into your kitchen. We ask that all countertop appliances must be removed to give us a full range of counter space. It is appreciated that you provide a clean space for us to cook, in which we will return the same. Thank you for your cooperation.
-Chef Ferragamo

> This allows all guests to be able to choose their threshold or even opt out
> completely.
> We have a very intricate way of plating and guest interaction that allows
> us to track + dose specific plates.
> It is very important to us that all guests are allowed the same exact
> experience, while also ensuring everyone's safety.
>
>
> *Rates per person*
> Normal | $200/4course, $240/8course, $260/12course
> Cannabis | $250/4course, $275/8course, $300/12course
>
>
> Guest count: min 5, max 20
>
> *The party size must be confirmed up to 5 days before the event takes
> place.
>
> *$20 up-charge per person for groups smaller than 5
>
>
> *Day of Event*
>
> We are a small team consisting of 1-3 chefs. Our team must have time to
> arrive 3-4hrs early prior to dining. Upon arrival our crew will spend a
> short time unloading equipment into your kitchen. We ask that all
> countertop appliances must be removed to give us a full range of counter
> space. It is appreciated that you provide a clean space for us to cook, in
> which we will return the same. Thank you for your cooperation.
>
> -Chef Ferragamo
>
>
>
>

dates.context: Re: Dinner Parties in Rockport/Camden: June 9 and June 16 Hey Hal, I hope you are ful
Parties in Rockport/Camden: June 9 and June 16 Hey Hal, I hope you are fulfilling all

fixtures.subject: Re: Dinner Parties in Rockport/Camden: June 9 and June 16
fixtures.body:
David,
Greetings.
There will be 6 for dinner on both June 9 and 16. There are no allergies.
The address is 50 Russell Avenue, Rockport, 04856.  The house is Aldermere.
Feel free to call me at 314-566-1490 to arrange payment.
Thanks.
Hal
Sent from my iPhone
> On May 28, 2022, at 1:04 PM, David Ferragamo <dfprivatechef@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> ï»¿
> Hey Hal,
>
> I hope you are fulfilling all your long-weekend plans! Would you have final head counts for your upcoming dates? Please include all allergies. Thank you.
>
> -Chef Ferragamo
>
>
>> On Wed, May 25, 2022 at 9:01 AM Hal Moore <halmoore1@aol.com> wrote:
>> Chef Ferragamo,
>>
>> I suspected as much, but thought I would ask.   Let's keep the plan as is for June 16.
>>
>> Thank  you.
>>
>> Hal
>>
>> In a message dated 5/24/2022 7:55:05 PM Central Standard Time, dfprivatechef@gmail.com writes:
>>
>> Hey Hal,
>>
>> I will not be able to fulfill this request within this short notice. My apologies.
>>
>> - Chef Ferragamo
>>
>>
>> On Tue, May 24, 2022, 5:59 PM Hal Moore <halmoore1@aol.com> wrote:
>> Chef Ferragamo,
>>
>> Greetings.  I hope all is well.
>>
>> Several of our guests have delayed their arrival by a day.  Would it be possible to change the dinner currently scheduled on Thursday, June 16 to the following day, Friday, June 17th?
>>
>> Thank you for considering this request.
>>
>> Best,
>> Hal
>>
>>
>> In a message dated 3/20/2022 1:52:55 PM Central Standard Time, halmoore1@aol.com writes:
>>
>> Chef Ferragamo,
>>
>> Greetings.  I hope you are enjoying/enjoyed your weekend, depending upon when you read this.
>>
>> Thank you for providing pork and chicken courses for review.  They all look great, but after careful consideration, we would like the stuffed pork loin substituted for the bison/beef on June 9th.  As an aside, we love the fact that the pork will be sourced from a local pork farmer, as we like supporting the local economy.
>>
>> In that vein, I'm arranging for a singer/songwriter from Wiscasset, Kat Logan, to play instrumental music during dinner and to sing afterward.
>>
>> Lastly, I'm not sure we have established a time for dinner.  I'm thinking around 6:30 if that works for you all.
>>
>> I hope your brackets are fairing better than mine.
>>
>> Best,
>> Hal
>>
>>
>>
>>
>> In a message dated 3/20/2022 7:01:40 AM Central Standard Time, dfprivatechef@gmail.com writes:
>>
>> Hey Hal,
>>
>> I'll gladly serve the soup first, no problem at all!
>> I have received your submission, thank you. All dinners will be paid 2 weeks prior to the event. I will be contacting you through Facebook business for payment on May 26 for the first dinner. The following covers all returns.
>>
>> If guest cancels up to 14 days before the experience: guest will be entirely reinboursed
>> If guest cancels between 14 - 7 days prior to the experience: chef is entitled to 50% of the reservationsâ value.
>> If guest cancels with less than 7 days in advance: chef is entitled to the entire coverage of the experience
>> If chef cancels: guest will be entirely reimbursement
>> I have 2 local pork farmers waiting on my word! I have created some pork/chicken courses for you. Please pick the dish you'd like to move forward with and which menu you'd prefer it to be on.
>>
>>  1. bbq pork or chicken burnt ends / corn + carmen peppers / summer pickles
>>
>> 2. brined + grilled pork chop or chicken thigh/ husk cherry jam / charred onion / potato + seasonal green
>>
>> 3. stuffed pork loin or chicken breast / zucchini + bronz fennel / plum
>>
>> Enjoy your Sunday! Chat soon.
>>
>> - Chef Ferragamo
>>
>>
>> On Wed, Mar 16, 2022, 5:59 PM <halmoore1@aol.com> wrote:
>> Chef Ferragamo,
>>
>> The menus look wonderful.  I've reviewed them with my wife and have just a couple of comments.   For the June 9th menu is there a reason that the salad precedes the soup?   I think we would prefer the soup coarse first.
>> Also, rather than have beef/bison both weeks could we substitute chicken or pork?  Hopefully, this request doesn't send you back to the drawing boards.
>> The menu planned for June 16 looks perfect as is?
>>
>> You may have noticed that I booked the June 16 via the link you provided.
>>
>> What are the next steps?  Also, I've lost track of how and when I pay you.
>>
>> We are very much looking forward to these 2 dinner parties.
>>
>> Thanks,
>> Hal
>>
>> In a message dated 3/16/2022 7:01:19 AM Central Standard Time, dfprivatechef@gmail.com writes:
>>
>> No worries! We can always arrange something in the future.
>> For the 16, you can feel free to book directly with me. Follow this link DF Private Chef and click "Book Appointment."
>>
>> I have created two separate menus for you. Let me know if you'd like to make any changes. Getting your specific requirements is important, thank you for the patience.
>> Please note that slight changes could be made due to seasonal opportunity.
>>
>> June 9
>> cucumber + bell pepper salad / smoked cherry goat cheese / white pine + black pepper vin / pecan
>>
>> basil salmon / zucchini + tomato gazpacho
>>
>>  bison or beef / pickled lilac peppercorns / asparagus / eggplant / sweet onion
>>
>>  rhubarb, strawberry + goat cheesecake / walnut crust
>>
>> June 16
>> radish, strawberry, tomato, fennel salad / manchego / crispy garlic scapes
>>
>>  poached crab / asparagus + pea / wheat tart / poppy seed dressing
>>
>>  beef / sweet potato / sauteed greens / peppers
>>
>>  sweet corn gelato / beet cocoa crumb / blueberry
>>
>>
>> It was fun to create these summer menus, we've been in such a winter braising mode! Looking forward to hearing from you.
>> -Chef Ferragamo
>>
>>
>> On Sat, Mar 12, 2022 at 9:36 AM <halmoore1@aol.com> wrote:
>> Chef Ferragamo,
>>
>> As much as I like the idea of the cannabis dining experience, I don't think my wife will approve, so I think it is best that we forego the THC.   I do want  a 4 course meal rather than family style though.   I would like different menus for June 9th and 16th, btw.   Do I need to initiate another dinner party on Take-a-chef for the 16th?
>>
>> I'm very excited about this.
>>
>> Best,
>> Hal
>>
>> In a message dated 3/12/2022 7:00:16 AM Central Standard Time, dfprivatechef@gmail.com writes:
>>
>> Hey Hal,
>>
>> Thank you for the picture of the kitchen. It helps us immesley for our preparation.
>> Our cannabis dining is different from what we are allowed on Take-a-chef. Thank you for showing interest.
>> Below is information regarding our dinners. Normally the Q+A is included but I have your feedback already. Please give me 2-3 days and I'll have a menu with options to refine available.
>>
>> You requested a 4-course meal, then it started to sound like you wanted family style once you learned about the cannabis addition.
>> When we hold our cannabis dinners, absolutely nothing is dosed beforehand. This allows all guests to be able to choose their threshold or even opt out completely.
>> We have a very intricate way of plating and guest interaction that allows us to track + dose specific plates.
>> It is very important to us that all guests are allowed the same exact experience, while also ensuring everyone's safety.
>>
>> Rates per person
>> Normal | $200/4course, $240/8course, $260/12course
>> Cannabis | $250/4course, $275/8course, $300/12course
>>
>> Guest count: min 5, max 20
>> *The party size must be confirmed up to 5 days before the event takes place.
>> *$20 up-charge per person for groups smaller than 5
>>
>> Day of Event
>>
>>  We are a small team consisting of 1-3 chefs. Our team must have time to arrive 3-4hrs early prior to dining. Upon arrival our crew will spend a short time unloading equipment into your kitchen. We ask that all countertop appliances must be removed to give us a full range of counter space. It is appreciated that you provide a clean space for us to cook, in which we will return the same. Thank you for your cooperation.
>>
>> -Chef Ferragamo
>>
>>

>>>> Cannabis | $250/4course, $275/8course, $300/12course
>>>>
>>>> Guest count: min 5, max 20
>>>> *The party size must be confirmed up to 5 days before the event takes place.
>>>> *$20 up-charge per person for groups smaller than 5
>>>>
>>>> Day of Event
>>>>
>>>>  We are a small team consisting of 1-3 chefs. Our team must have time to arrive 3-4hrs early prior to dining. Upon arrival our crew will spend a short time unloading equipment into your kitchen. We ask that all countertop appliances must be removed to give us a full range of counter space. It is appreciated that you provide a clean space for us to cook, in which we will return the same. Thank you for your cooperation.
>>>>
>>>> -Chef Ferragamo
>>>>

fixtures.subject: Re: Dinner Parties in Rockport/Camden: June 9 and June 16
fixtures.body:
Hey Hal,
Thank you for your email. We have sent your invoices in the files attached.
As for the day of the event, we will arrive 4hrs prior to dining. Our ETA
will be around 2:30pm. Please let me know if you have any questions.
- Chef Ferragamo
We are only accepting Venmo payment or cash at this time. Thank you.
Please follow link or scan QR code to Venmo for Payment
https://venmo.com/DFPrivateChef
<https://venmo.com/code?user_id=2147263816138752647&created=1651293814>

>>>> Kat Logan, to play instrumental music during dinner and to sing afterward.
>>>>
>>>> Lastly, I'm not sure we have established a time for dinner.  I'm
>>>> thinking around 6:30 if that works for you all.
>>>>
>>>> I hope your brackets are fairing better than mine.
>>>>
>>>> Best,
>>>> Hal
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> In a message dated 3/20/2022 7:01:40 AM Central Standard Time,
>>>> dfprivatechef@gmail.com writes:
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Hey Hal,
>>>>
>>>> I'll gladly serve the soup first, no problem at all!
>>>> I have received your submission, thank you. All dinners will be paid 2
>>>> weeks prior to the event. I will be contacting you through Facebook
>>>> business for payment on May 26 for the first dinner. The following covers
>>>> all returns.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>    - If guest cancels up to 14 days before the experience: guest will
>>>>    be entirely reinboursed
>>>>    - If guest cancels between 14 - 7 days prior to the experience:
>>>>    chef is entitled to 50% of the reservationsâ value.
>>>>    - If guest cancels with less than 7 days in advance: chef is
>>>>    entitled to the entire coverage of the experience
>>>>    - If chef cancels: guest will be entirely reimbursement
>>>>
>>>> I have 2 local pork farmers waiting on my word! I have created some
>>>> pork/chicken courses for you. Please pick the dish you'd like to
>>>> move forward with and which menu you'd prefer it to be on.
>>>>
>>>>  1. bbq pork or chicken burnt ends / corn + carmen peppers / summer
>>>> pickles
>>>>
>>>> 2. brined + grilled pork chop or chicken thigh/ husk cherry jam /
>>>> charred onion / potato + seasonal green
>>>>
>>>> 3. stuffed pork loin or chicken breast / zucchini + bronz fennel / plum
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Enjoy your Sunday! Chat soon.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> - Chef Ferragamo
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> On Wed, Mar 16, 2022, 5:59 PM <halmoore1@aol.com> wrote:
>>>>
>>>> Chef Ferragamo,
>>>>
>>>> The menus look wonderful.  I've reviewed them with my wife and have
>>>> just a couple of comments.   For the June 9th menu is there a reason that
>>>> the salad precedes the soup?   I think we would prefer the soup coarse
>>>> first.
>>>> Also, rather than have beef/bison both weeks could we substitute
>>>> chicken or pork?  Hopefully, this request doesn't send you back to the
>>>> drawing boards.
>>>> The menu planned for June 16 looks perfect as is?
>>>>
>>>> You may have noticed that I booked the June 16 via the link you
>>>> provided.
>>>>
>>>> What are the next steps?  Also, I've lost track of how and when I pay
>>>> you.
>>>>
>>>> We are very much looking forward to these 2 dinner parties.
>>>>
>>>> Thanks,
>>>> Hal
>>>>
>>>> In a message dated 3/16/2022 7:01:19 AM Central Standard Time,
>>>> dfprivatechef@gmail.com writes:
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> No worries! We can always arrange something in the future.
>>>> For the 16, you can feel free to book directly with me. Follow this
>>>> link DF Private Che <https://www.facebook.com/ChefFerragamo>f and
>>>> click "Book Appointment."
>>>>
>>>> I have created two separate menus for you. Let me know if you'd like to
>>>> make any changes. Getting your specific requirements is important, thank
>>>> you for the patience.
>>>> Please note that slight changes could be made due to seasonal
>>>> opportunity.
>>>>
>>>> *June 9*
>>>>
>>>> cucumber + bell pepper salad / smoked cherry goat cheese / white pine +
>>>> black pepper vin / pecan
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> basil salmon / zucchini + tomato gazpacho
>>>>
>>>> bison or beef / pickled lilac peppercorns / asparagus / eggplant /
>>>> sweet onion
>>>>
>>>> rhubarb, strawberry + goat cheesecake / walnut crust
>>>>
>>>> *June 16*
>>>>
>>>> radish, strawberry, tomato, fennel salad / manchego / crispy garlic
>>>> scapes
>>>>
>>>> poached crab / asparagus + pea / wheat tart / poppy seed dressing
>>>>
>>>> beef / sweet potato / sauteed greens / peppers
>>>>
>>>> sweet corn gelato / beet cocoa crumb / blueberry
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> It was fun to create these summer menus, we've been in such a winter
>>>> braising mode! Looking forward to hearing from you.
>>>> -Chef Ferragamo
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> On Sat, Mar 12, 2022 at 9:36 AM <halmoore1@aol.com> wrote:
>>>>
>>>> Chef Ferragamo,
>>>>
>>>> As much as I like the idea of the cannabis dining experience, I don't
>>>> think my wife will approve, so I think it is best that we forego the THC.
>>>>  I do want  a 4 course meal rather than family style though.   I would like
>>>> different menus for June 9th and 16th, btw.   Do I need to initiate another
>>>> dinner party on Take-a-chef for the 16th?
>>>>
>>>> I'm very excited about this.
>>>>
>>>> Best,
>>>> Hal
>>>>
>>>> In a message dated 3/12/2022 7:00:16 AM Central Standard Time,
>>>> dfprivatechef@gmail.com writes:
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Hey Hal,
>>>>
>>>> Thank you for the picture of the kitchen. It helps us immesley for our
>>>> preparation.
>>>> Our cannabis dining is different from what we are allowed on
>>>> Take-a-chef. Thank you for showing interest.
>>>> Below is information regarding our dinners. Normally the Q+A is
>>>> included but I have your feedback already. Please give me 2-3 days and I'll
>>>> have a menu with options to refine available.
>>>>
>>>> You requested a 4-course meal, then it started to sound like you wanted
>>>> family style once you learned about the cannabis addition.
>>>> When we hold our cannabis dinners, absolutely nothing is dosed
>>>> beforehand. This allows all guests to be able to choose their threshold or
>>>> even opt out completely.
>>>> We have a very intricate way of plating and guest interaction that
>>>> allows us to track + dose specific plates.
>>>> It is very important to us that all guests are allowed the same exact
>>>> experience, while also ensuring everyone's safety.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> *Rates per person*
>>>> Normal | $200/4course, $240/8course, $260/12course
>>>> Cannabis | $250/4course, $275/8course, $300/12course
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Guest count: min 5, max 20
>>>>
>>>> *The party size must be confirmed up to 5 days before the event takes
>>>> place.
>>>>
>>>> *$20 up-charge per person for groups smaller than 5
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> *Day of Event*
>>>>
>>>> We are a small team consisting of 1-3 chefs. Our team must have time to
>>>> arrive 3-4hrs early prior to dining. Upon arrival our crew will spend a
>>>> short time unloading equipment into your kitchen. We ask that all
>>>> countertop appliances must be removed to give us a full range of counter
>>>> space. It is appreciated that you provide a clean space for us to cook, in
>>>> which we will return the same. Thank you for your cooperation.
>>>>
>>>> -Chef Ferragamo
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>
>>>>

fixtures.subject: Re: Dinner Parties in Rockport/Camden: June 9 and June 16
fixtures.body:
Hey Hal,
Hope you're enjoying this cozy feasting weather! Your payment was received,
thank you. Venmo is a new platform for us too, glad we got it figured out.
Our new ETA has now changed to 3:30, no longer 2:30. Thank you for
understanding.
We will have very minimal loading of equipment upon arrival. We will be in
touch upon arrival. See you soon!
- Chef Ferragamo

I see I provided the address in this thread.  It is easy to miss so feel free to call me at 314-566-1490 and Iâll walk to Russell Avenue to flag you down.
Best,
Hal

>>>>>>>>>>> basil salmon / zucchini + tomato gazpacho
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>  bison or beef / pickled lilac peppercorns / asparagus / eggplant / sweet onion
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>  rhubarb, strawberry + goat cheesecake / walnut crust
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> June 16
>>>>>>>>>>> radish, strawberry, tomato, fennel salad / manchego / crispy garlic scapes
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>  poached crab / asparagus + pea / wheat tart / poppy seed dressing
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>  beef / sweet potato / sauteed greens / peppers
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>  sweet corn gelato / beet cocoa crumb / blueberry
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> It was fun to create these summer menus, we've been in such a winter braising mode! Looking forward to hearing from you.
>>>>>>>>>>> -Chef Ferragamo
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> On Sat, Mar 12, 2022 at 9:36 AM <halmoore1@aol.com> wrote:
>>>>>>>>>>> Chef Ferragamo,
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> As much as I like the idea of the cannabis dining experience, I don't think my wife will approve, so I think it is best that we forego the THC.   I do want  a 4 course meal rather than family style though.   I would like different menus for June 9th and 16th, btw.   Do I need to initiate another dinner party on Take-a-chef for the 16th?
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> I'm very excited about this.
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> Best,
>>>>>>>>>>> Hal
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> In a message dated 3/12/2022 7:00:16 AM Central Standard Time, dfprivatechef@gmail.com writes:
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> Hey Hal,
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> Thank you for the picture of the kitchen. It helps us immesley for our preparation.
>>>>>>>>>>> Our cannabis dining is different from what we are allowed on Take-a-chef. Thank you for showing interest.
>>>>>>>>>>> Below is information regarding our dinners. Normally the Q+A is included but I have your feedback already. Please give me 2-3 days and I'll have a menu with options to refine available.
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> You requested a 4-course meal, then it started to sound like you wanted family style once you learned about the cannabis addition.
>>>>>>>>>>> When we hold our cannabis dinners, absolutely nothing is dosed beforehand. This allows all guests to be able to choose their threshold or even opt out completely.
>>>>>>>>>>> We have a very intricate way of plating and guest interaction that allows us to track + dose specific plates.
>>>>>>>>>>> It is very important to us that all guests are allowed the same exact experience, while also ensuring everyone's safety.
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> Rates per person
>>>>>>>>>>> Normal | $200/4course, $240/8course, $260/12course
>>>>>>>>>>> Cannabis | $250/4course, $275/8course, $300/12course
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> Guest count: min 5, max 20
>>>>>>>>>>> *The party size must be confirmed up to 5 days before the event takes place.
>>>>>>>>>>> *$20 up-charge per person for groups smaller than 5
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> Day of Event
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>  We are a small team consisting of 1-3 chefs. Our team must have time to arrive 3-4hrs early prior to dining. Upon arrival our crew will spend a short time unloading equipment into your kitchen. We ask that all countertop appliances must be removed to give us a full range of counter space. It is appreciated that you provide a clean space for us to cook, in which we will return the same. Thank you for your cooperation.
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> -Chef Ferragamo
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>
>>>>>
>>

fixtures.subject: Inquiry
fixtures.body:
Hi Chef David,
I hope you are doing well!
I requested to book with you for June 28th through your website, and was wandering if this could be a possibility? I just wanted to check in and see if my request went through.
Thank you!
Mia
Sent from my iPhone

dates.context: well! I requested to book with you for June 28th through your website, and was wandering

fixtures.subject: Re: Inquiry
fixtures.body:
Hi Mia,
Thanks so much for checking in, and I appreciate your patience while I
catch up. Iâd love to cook for you on June 28th for Alanaâs 30th. That
sounds like a fun one.
Iâll start working on some menu options and will have them to you later
this week. I always build menus custom to the group, but if you already
have a direction youâre leaning toward, feel free to let me know.
Hereâs the pricing for groups of three or more:
$155 per person for three courses
$185 per person for four courses
$215 per person for five courses
I also offer large tasting menus, up to 15 plus courses. This would have
different rates.
I work closely with local farms, and I also forage and grow a lot of my own
ingredients. I try to keep most dinners as local and seasonal as possible
to really represent the area and the time of year.
I saw your notes. Weâll avoid crab, lamb, and anything not
pregnancy-friendly. Iâll keep it fun and flavorful without compromising on
safety.
Looking forward to putting something together for you.
Best regards,
Chef Ferragamo
PF Private Chef

fixtures.subject: Re: Inquiry
fixtures.body:
Hi Chef Ferragamo,
Thank you so much for the thoughtful message. We are really excited to work
with you for Alanaâs birthday celebration.
One quick note â one member of our group is vegan, gluten sensitive, and
also has sensitivities to potatoes, sweet potatoes, turmeric, and
watermelon. We would really appreciate accommodations for that. If this
changes the menu for all guests no worries, and disregard the above (that
person is me so no worries haha)
Other than that, we do not have any specific culinary preferences, so
please feel free to create whatever you would like. We trust your
expertise!
We are also still deciding between doing three or five courses. If it is
not too much trouble, would you mind sharing your ideas for both options so
we can choose from there?
Looking forward to seeing what you come up with! Thank you again!
Best regards,
Mia Corrado

fixtures.subject: Re: Inquiry
fixtures.body:
Thank you for gathering your groupâs allergies and preferences. It makes my
job a lot easier.
Iâve attached a custom summer menu built with everything in mind. Thereâs
no crab, lamb, fish, cold cuts, raw foods, or anything unpasteurized. I
also avoided sweet potatoes, turmeric, and watermelon, and Iâve included a
solid vegan and gluten-free option. Totally no problem! Nobody would know
the difference. All the food will be delicious.
Does Alana have any foods sheâs obsessed with? Itâs always fun to throw in
a surprise.
Youâll see the full list of courses below. You can pick one veg, one main,
and one dessert if you're leaning toward a three-course dinner, or choose
up to five dishes from anywhere on the menu. We do suggest limiting the
mains to two just to keep it smooth. If you end up wanting three mains,
that usually comes with an upcharge, but happy to throw it in on the house
if thatâs what you go with. Two desserts is always very fun!
I have you down for a 5:30 serving time. I typically arrive two hours
before to set up.
Let me know what sounds good and Iâll take it from there.
Chef Ferragamo
---
New England Summer
All dishes have vegan versions available. All vegan dishes are cooked in
olive oil. All vegan dairy will be house-made.
VEG
â¢ SautÃ©ed snap pea and broccolini salad with brown butter vinaigrette,
manchego
â¢ Grilled stone fruit, arugula, house-made vegan ricotta, preserved pepper
vinaigrette
â¢ Summer salad with blueberry balsamic vinaigrette and fresh mozzarella
â¢ Chopped beet, spinach, and charred onion salad with black pepper
blueberry vinaigrette, dried lime balm, and goat cheese
â¢ Tomato gazpacho with crispy shallot oil, basil, and pruned blueberry
capers
â¢ Smoked peppers filled with goat cream cheese, black walnuts, and lilac
garlic vinaigrette
â¢ Strawberry carpaccio with charred corn relish, goat cheese, and lots of
herbs
â¢ Tomato, arugula, and herb salad with charred strawberry balsamic
vinaigrette, mint, and tarragon
MAIN
â¢ Spice-rubbed filet with basil butter, grilled tomatoes, warm broth, and
crispy potato pavÃ©
â¢ Aged grilled ribeye with rosemary sherry sauce, summer squash, zucchini,
and onion
â¢ Marinated grilled NY sirloin with smoked tomato butter, creamed spinach,
and crispy garlic
â¢ Blackberry smoked and braised short rib with grilled vegetable salad and
cornbread
â¢ Chicken roulade stuffed with tomato, basil, and asparagus with crispy
potato
â¢ Roasted chicken with herb jus, burnt onion, turnips, and marinated cherry
DESSERT
â¢ Basque cheesecake with sweet fern crust and marinated berries
â¢ Peach custard tart with white tea gelato
â¢ Strawberry shortcake with candied lime balm
â¢ Chocolate and hazelnut bread pudding with chocolate ganache and raspberry
â¢ Chocoflan with chocolate chip gelato
â¢ Chocolate lava cake with seasonal fruit and gelato

fixtures.subject: Re: Inquiry
fixtures.body:
Hi Chef Ferragamo,
Thank you so much for sending over these incredible menu ideas â everything
looks absolutely amazing, and so generous! We were all so impressed by the
creativity and variety, and we can already tell this will be such a special
dinner.
I just had a couple of small thoughts. Would it be possible to offer a few
more appetizer options that are not salad-based, just to balance things out
a bit? Everything sounds delicious, and we will probably still pick one or
two of the vegan options you suggested, but no need to worry about
providing vegan and gluten-free options across the board â we are happy to
enjoy the full range of dishes.
Also, if itâs possible, we would love to include a little surprise dessert
â maybe something chocolate and hazelnutâfocused, (ur bread pudding sounds
amazing, but my sister isnât a bread pudding fan sadly ð) since my sister
isnât a huge fan of fruit-forward desserts.
Thank you again â we are so excited and canât wait to see what you create!

fixtures.subject: Re: Inquiry
fixtures.body:
Hi Mia,
I really appreciate your patience while I updated your options. I know the
date is coming up quickly, and I hope you're happy with these. I'd love to
hear your thoughts. I can make any changes you'd like!
Again, any of the main courses you've seen will have a structured vegan
version. For example, the braised short rib would be swapped for a braised
celeriac root, and so on.
I know I got the direction for chocolate and hazelnut, if you have any
berries or fruit in mines that your group loves please let me know.
Veg
â¢ Zucchini roulade with marinated tomatoes and basil
â¢ Fried pickles with fermented garlic aioli and buttermilk chive dip
â¢ Grilled brioche toast with whipped stracciatella, peach jelly, smoked
marinated peppers, and crispy shallots
â¢ Honeydew melon and cucumber gazpacho with lilac vinegar, bronze fennel,
fresh mint, and toasted fennel seed oil
â¢ Grilled shishito peppers tossed with smoked tomato butter, served with
giardiniera ponzu
â¢ Grilled flatbread with whipped feta, savory marinated blueberries,
pickled shallot, fresh herbs, and crunchy garlic walnuts
Desserts
â¢ Chocolate mousse tart with hazelnut crust
â¢ Chocolate hazelnut tiramisu
â¢ Ice cream sandwich with hazelnut cookie, chocolate gelato with chocolate
chips
â¢ Nostalgic dirt pudding with hazelnut and chocolate
- Chef Ferragamo

fixtures.subject: Re: Inquiry
fixtures.body:
Hi Chef Ferragamo,
Thank you so much for putting time and effort into this amazing meal. I
really appreciate all your work you have done in your busy schedule.
Unfortunately, I just heard word from my sister, she explained that she was
a bit worried about having a decadent meal in her last trimester, because
of her sensitive stomach. Iâm so sorry to tell you this last minute, I just
have received this information now.
That being said, I would love to keep in contact with you, in order to hire
you for a meal with less restrictions. Your food looks amazing!
Thank you again,
Mia

fixtures.subject: Re: Inquiry
fixtures.body:
Hi Mia,
Thanks so much for letting me know, and no worries at all, I totally
understand. I hope the rest of the pregnancy goes well!
Iâd be happy to stay in touch and would love to cook for you in the future
when the timing and restrictions feel right. Just reach out anytime. ð
Best Regards,
Chef Ferragamo
